revision.comment: Auto generating page. date placed far in the past so it is attributed to Immanuelle and FireflyBot sends them messages if it is ever imported to Wikipedia.
revision.text: #redirect[[en:Kego Shrine]] <includeonly>[[Category:Erroneous transclusions of Kego Shrine]]</includeonly> <noinclude>[[Category:New automatic wikipedia redirects]]</noinclude>

revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: '{{神社| 名称=警固神社| 所在地=福岡県福岡市中央区天神2-2-20| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|35|15|N|130|23|59|E|scale:30000}}| 祭...'
revision.text:
{{神社| 名称=警固神社| 所在地=福岡県福岡市中央区天神2-2-20| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|35|15|N|130|23|59|E|scale:30000}}| 祭神=警固大神（神直毘神・大直毘神・八十禍津日神）| 創建=仲哀天皇9年| 社格=県社| 例祭=[[7月19日]]以降の土曜・日曜日（夏越し祭）}} '''警固神社'''（けごじんじゃ）は、[[福岡県]][[福岡市]]にある[[神社]]である。旧[[社格]]は[[県社]]。 ==祭神== 本殿に警固大神（[[直毘神|神直毘神]]・[[直毘神|大直毘神]]・[[禍津日神|八十禍津日神]]）、相殿に[[賀茂建角身命|建角身神]]・[[トヨタマヒメ|豊玉姫命]]・[[神功皇后]]・[[応神天皇]]を祀る。 ==歴史== 社伝では神功皇后による[[三韓征伐]]の際、皇后の船団を守護し勝利に導いた警固大神を福崎（現在の[[福岡城]]本丸周辺）の地に祀ったのが始まりとされる。[[慶長]]6年（[[1601年]]）福岡城築城の際に下警固村（現在の[[福岡市]][[中央区 (福岡市)|中央区]]天神周辺）に移され、[[慶長]]13年（[[1608年]]）に福岡城主[[黒田長政]]によって現在の場所に社殿が造営された。[[大正]]5年（[[1916年]]）、県社に列格した。 社名及び周辺の地名である『警固』はかつて[[鴻臚館]]付近にあったとされる[[大宰府]]政庁の役所『警固所』に由来する。 ==施設== *拝殿 *本殿 *大鳥居 *神門 *今益稲荷神社 *神徳殿（貸し会場） また、警固公園は当神社の隣にある。 ==交通== [[西日本鉄道]][[大牟田線]][[西鉄福岡（天神）駅]] 徒歩1分
[[福岡市地下鉄]][[天神駅]] 徒歩3分
西鉄バス『天神警固神社[[三越]]前』 徒歩1分
==外部リンク== *[http://www.kegojinja.or.jp/ 警固神社]（公式サイト） [[Category:福岡県の神社|けごじんじゃ]] [[Category:府県社|けごじんじゃ]] {{shinto-stub}}

{{神社| 名称=警固神社| 所在地=福岡県福岡市中央区天神2-2-20| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|35|15|N|130|23|59|E|scale:30000}}| 祭神=警固大神（神直毘神・大直毘神・八十禍津日神）| 創建=仲哀天皇9年| 社格=県社| 例祭=[[7月19日]]以降の土曜・日曜日（夏越し祭）}} '''警固神社'''（けごじんじゃ）は、[[福岡県]][[福岡市]]にある[[神社]]である。旧[[社格]]は[[県社]]。 ==祭神== 本殿に警固大神（[[直毘神|神直毘神]]・[[直毘神|大直毘神]]・[[禍津日神|八十禍津日神]]）、相殿に[[賀茂建角身命|建角身神]]・[[トヨタマヒメ|豊玉姫命]]・[[神功皇后]]・[[応神天皇]]を祀る。 ==歴史== 社伝では神功皇后による[[三韓征伐]]の際、皇后の船団を守護し勝利に導いた警固大神を福崎（現在の[[福岡城]]本丸周辺）の地に祀ったのが始まりとされる。[[慶長]]6年（[[1601年]]）福岡城築城の際に下警固村（現在の[[福岡市]][[中央区 (福岡市)|中央区]]天神周辺）に移され、[[慶長]]13年（[[1608年]]）に福岡城主[[黒田長政]]によって現在の場所に社殿が造営された。[[大正]]5年（[[1916年]]）、県社に列格した。 社名及び周辺の地名である『警固』はかつて[[鴻臚館]]にあった役所『警固所』に由来する。 ==施設== *拝殿 *本殿 *大鳥居 *神門 *今益稲荷神社 *神徳殿（貸し会場） また、警固公園は当神社の隣にある。 ==交通== [[西日本鉄道]][[大牟田線]][[西鉄福岡（天神）駅]] 徒歩1分
[[福岡市地下鉄]][[天神駅]] 徒歩3分
西鉄バス『天神警固神社[[三越]]前』 徒歩1分
==外部リンク== *[http://www.kegojinja.or.jp/ 警固神社]（公式サイト） [[Category:福岡県の神社|けごじんじゃ]] [[Category:府県社|けごじんじゃ]] {{shinto-stub}}

[[福岡市地下鉄]][[天神駅]] 徒歩3分

{{神社 |名称=警固神社 |画像 = [[File:Kegojinja01.jpg|300px|警固神社参道]] |所在地=福岡県福岡市中央区天神2-2-20 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|35|15|N|130|23|59|E|scale:30000}} |祭神=警固大神（神直毘神・大直毘神・八十禍津日神） |創建=仲哀天皇9年 |社格=県社 |例祭=[[7月19日]]以降の土曜・日曜日（夏越し祭） }} '''警固神社'''（けごじんじゃ）は、[[福岡県]][[福岡市]][[中央区 (福岡市)|中央区]]にある[[神社]]である。旧[[社格]]は[[県社]]。 ==祭神== 本殿に警固大神（[[直毘神|神直毘神]]・[[直毘神|大直毘神]]・[[禍津日神|八十禍津日神]]）、相殿に[[賀茂建角身命|建角身神]]・[[トヨタマヒメ|豊玉姫命]]・[[神功皇后]]・[[応神天皇]]を祀る。 ==歴史== 社伝では神功皇后による[[三韓征伐]]の際、皇后の船団を守護し勝利に導いた警固大神を福崎（現在の[[福岡城]]本丸周辺）の地に祀ったのが始まりとされる。[[慶長]]6年（[[1601年]]）福岡城築城の際に下警固村（現在の[[福岡市]][[中央区 (福岡市)|中央区]][[天神 (福岡市)|天神]]周辺）に移され、[[慶長]]13年（[[1608年]]）に福岡城主[[黒田長政]]によって現在の場所に社殿が造営された。[[大正]]5年（[[1916年]]）、県社に列格した。 社名及び周辺の地名である『警固』はかつて[[鴻臚館]]にあった役所『警固所』に由来する。 ==施設== [[File:Kegojinja02.jpg|thumb|240px||拝殿]] *拝殿 *本殿 *大鳥居 *神門 *針乃碑 *今益稲荷神社 *神徳殿（貸し会場） [[警固公園]]は神社北隣に接する。 ==交通== *[[西日本鉄道]][[西鉄天神大牟田線|天神大牟田線]][[西鉄福岡（天神）駅]] 徒歩1分 *[[福岡市交通局|福岡市地下鉄]][[福岡市地下鉄空港線|空港線]][[天神駅]] 徒歩3分 *[[西鉄バス]]「天神警固神社[[三越]]前」 徒歩1分

{{神社 |名称=警固神社 |画像 = [[File:Kegojinja01.jpg|300px|警固神社参道]] |所在地=福岡県福岡市中央区天神2-2-20 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|35|15|N|130|23|59|E|scale:30000}} |祭神=警固大神（神直毘神・大直毘神・八十禍津日神） |創建=仲哀天皇9年 |社格=県社 |例祭=[[7月19日]]以降の土曜・日曜日（夏越し祭） }} '''警固神社'''（けごじんじゃ）は、[[福岡県]][[福岡市]][[中央区 (福岡市)|中央区]]にある[[神社]]である。旧[[社格]]は[[県社]]。 ==祭神== 本殿に警固大神（[[直毘神|神直毘神]]・[[直毘神|大直毘神]]・[[禍津日神|八十禍津日神]]）、相殿に[[賀茂建角身命|建角身神]]・[[トヨタマヒメ|豊玉姫命]]・[[神功皇后]]・[[応神天皇]]を祀る。 ==歴史== 社伝では神功皇后による[[三韓征伐]]の際、皇后の船団を守護し勝利に導いた警固大神を福崎（現在の[[福岡城]]本丸周辺）の地に祀ったのが始まりとされる。[[慶長]]6年（[[1601年]]）福岡城築城の際に下警固村（現在の[[福岡市]][[中央区 (福岡市)|中央区]][[天神 (福岡市)|天神]]周辺）に移され、[[慶長]]13年（[[1608年]]）に福岡城主[[黒田長政]]によって現在の場所に社殿が造営された。[[大正]]5年（[[1916年]]）、県社に列格した。 社名及び周辺の地名である『警固』はかつて[[鴻臚館]]にあった役所『警固所』に由来する。 ==施設== [[File:Kegojinja02.jpg|thumb|240px||拝殿]] [[File:Kego Shrine in Fukuoka 20131229.jpg|thumb|240px|神門]] *拝殿 *本殿 *大鳥居 *神門 *針乃碑 *今益稲荷神社 *神徳殿（貸し会場） [[警固公園]]は神社北隣に接する。 ==交通== *[[西日本鉄道]][[西鉄天神大牟田線|天神大牟田線]][[西鉄福岡（天神）駅]] （南口）徒歩1分 *[[福岡市交通局|福岡市地下鉄]][[福岡市地下鉄空港線|空港線]][[天神駅]] 徒歩7分 *福岡市地下鉄[[福岡市地下鉄七隈線|七隈線]][[天神南駅]] 徒歩4分 *[[西鉄バス]]「天神警固神社・[[三越]]前」 徒歩1分

{{神社 |名称=警固神社 |画像 = [[File:Torii of Kego Shrine.JPG|300px|警固神社参道]] |所在地=福岡県福岡市中央区天神2-2-20 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|35|15|N|130|23|59|E|type:landmark_region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} |祭神=警固大神（神直毘神・大直毘神・八十禍津日神） |創建=仲哀天皇9年 |社格=県社 |例祭=[[7月19日]]以降の土曜・日曜日（夏越し祭） }} {{osm box|w|384542332}}
'''警固神社'''（けごじんじゃ）は、[[福岡県]][[福岡市]][[中央区 (福岡市)|中央区]]にある[[神社]]である。旧[[社格]]は[[県社]]。
==祭神== 本殿に警固大神（[[直毘神|神直毘神]]・[[直毘神|大直毘神]]・[[禍津日神|八十禍津日神]]）、相殿に[[賀茂建角身命|建角身神]]・[[トヨタマヒメ|豊玉姫命]]・[[神功皇后]]・[[応神天皇]]を祀る。
==歴史== 社伝では神功皇后による[[三韓征伐]]の際、皇后の船団を守護し勝利に導いた警固大神を福崎（現在の[[福岡城]]本丸周辺）の地に祀ったのが始まりとされる。[[慶長]]6年（[[1601年]]）福岡城築城の際に下警固村（現在の[[福岡市]][[中央区 (福岡市)|中央区]][[天神 (福岡市)|天神]]周辺）に移され、[[慶長]]13年（[[1608年]]）に福岡城主[[黒田長政]]によって現在の場所に社殿が造営された。[[大正]]5年（[[1916年]]）、県社に列格した。
社名及び周辺の地名である『警固』はかつて[[鴻臚館]]にあった役所『警固所』に由来する。
==施設== [[File:Haiden of Kego Shrine 2.jpg|thumb|240px||拝殿]] [[File:Shimmon Gate of Kego Shrine.JPG|thumb|240px|神門]] *拝殿 *本殿 *大鳥居 *神門 *針乃碑 *今益稲荷神社 *神徳殿（貸し会場）
[[警固公園]]は神社北隣に接する。
==交通== *[[西日本鉄道]][[西鉄天神大牟田線|天神大牟田線]][[西鉄福岡（天神）駅]] （南口）徒歩1分 *[[福岡市交通局|福岡市地下鉄]][[福岡市地下鉄空港線|空港線]][[天神駅]] 徒歩7分 *福岡市地下鉄[[福岡市地下鉄七隈線|七隈線]][[天神南駅]] 徒歩4分 *[[西鉄バス]]「天神警固神社・[[三越]]前」 徒歩1分

==交通== *[[西日本鉄道]][[西鉄天神大牟田線|天神大牟田線]][[西鉄福岡（天神）駅]] （南口）徒歩1分 *[[福岡市交通局|福岡市地下鉄]][[福岡市地下鉄空港線|空港線]][[天神駅]] 徒歩7分 *福岡市地下鉄[[福岡市地下鉄七隈線|七隈線]][[天神南駅]] 徒歩4分 *[[西鉄バス]]「天神警固神社・[[三越]]前」 徒歩1分

==外部リンク== {{Commonscat|Kego-jinja}} *[http://www.kegojinja.or.jp/ 警固神社]（公式サイト） {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:けこしんしや}} [[Category:福岡県の神社]] [[Category:福岡県の旧県社]] [[Category:福岡天神]] [[Category:福岡市中央区の歴史]] [[Category:福岡市中央区の建築物]]

{{神社 |名称=警固神社 |画像 = [[File:Torii of Kego Shrine.JPG|300px|警固神社参道]] |所在地=福岡県福岡市中央区天神2-2-20 | 緯度度 = 33 | 緯度分 = 35 | 緯度秒 = 16 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 130 |経度分 = 23 | 経度秒 = 59.7 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 地図 = Fukuoka city |祭神=警固大神（神直毘神・大直毘神・八十禍津日神） |創建=仲哀天皇9年 |社格=県社 |例祭=[[7月19日]]以降の土曜・日曜日（夏越し祭） }} {{osm box|w|384542332}}
'''警固神社'''（けごじんじゃ）は、[[福岡県]][[福岡市]][[中央区 (福岡市)|中央区]]にある[[神社]]である。旧[[社格]]は[[県社]]。
==祭神== 本殿に警固大神（[[直毘神|神直毘神]]・[[直毘神|大直毘神]]・[[禍津日神|八十禍津日神]]）、相殿に[[賀茂建角身命|建角身神]]・[[トヨタマヒメ|豊玉姫命]]・[[神功皇后]]・[[応神天皇]]を祀る。
==歴史== 社伝では神功皇后による[[三韓征伐]]の際、皇后の船団を守護し勝利に導いた警固大神を福崎（現在の[[福岡城]]本丸周辺）の地に祀ったのが始まりとされる。[[慶長]]6年（[[1601年]]）福岡城築城の際に下警固村（現在の[[福岡市]][[中央区 (福岡市)|中央区]][[天神 (福岡市)|天神]]周辺）に移され、[[慶長]]13年（[[1608年]]）に福岡城主[[黒田長政]]によって現在の場所に社殿が造営された。[[大正]]5年（[[1916年]]）、県社に列格した。
社名及び周辺の地名である『警固』はかつて[[鴻臚館]]にあった役所『警固所』に由来する。
==施設== [[File:Haiden of Kego Shrine 2.jpg|thumb|240px|拝殿]] [[File:Shimmon Gate of Kego Shrine.JPG|thumb|240px|神門]] *拝殿 *本殿 *大鳥居 *神門 *針乃碑 *今益稲荷神社 *神徳殿（貸し会場）
[[警固公園]]は神社北隣に接する。
==交通== *[[西日本鉄道]][[西鉄天神大牟田線|天神大牟田線]][[西鉄福岡（天神）駅]] （南口）徒歩1分 *[[福岡市交通局|福岡市地下鉄]][[福岡市地下鉄空港線|空港線]][[天神駅]] 徒歩7分 *福岡市地下鉄[[福岡市地下鉄七隈線|七隈線]][[天神南駅]] 徒歩4分 *[[西鉄バス]]「天神警固神社・[[三越]]前」 徒歩1分
== 写真 == <Gallery> File:Stone monument at Kego-jinja.JPG File:Stone animal monument at Kego-jinja.jpg </Gallery>

revision.comment: /* 祭神 */ 公式ホームページの記載に合わせた。
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称=警固神社 |画像 = [[File:Torii of Kego Shrine.JPG|250px|警固神社参道]]
鳥居と拝殿 |所在地=[[福岡県]][[福岡市]][[中央区 (福岡市)|中央区]]天神2-2-20 | 緯度度 = 33 | 緯度分 = 35 | 緯度秒 = 16 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 130 |経度分 = 23 | 経度秒 = 59.7 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 地図 = Fukuoka city |祭神=警固大神（[[神直毘神]]・[[大直毘神]]・[[八十禍津日神]]） |創建=[[仲哀天皇]]9年 |社格=旧[[県社]] |例祭=[[7月19日]]以降の土曜・日曜日（夏越し祭） }} {{osm box|n|384542332}} '''警固神社'''（けごじんじゃ）は、[[福岡県]][[福岡市]][[中央区 (福岡市)|中央区]]にある[[神社]]である。旧[[社格]]は[[県社]]。 ==祭神== 警固大神（神直毘神・大直毘神・八十禍津日神の三柱） 　[[直毘神|神直毘神]]（かみなほびのかみ） 　[[直毘神|大直毘神]]（おほなほびのかみ） 　[[禍津日神|八十禍津日神]]（やそまがつひのかみ） ==歴史== 社伝では神功皇后による[[三韓征伐]]の際、皇后の船団を守護し勝利に導いた警固大神を福崎（現在の[[福岡城]]本丸周辺）の地に祀ったのが始まりとされる。[[慶長]]6年（[[1601年]]）福岡城築城の際に下警固村（現在の[[福岡市]][[中央区 (福岡市)|中央区]][[天神 (福岡市)|天神]]周辺）に移され、[[慶長]]13年（[[1608年]]）に福岡城主[[黒田長政]]によって現在の場所に社殿が造営された。[[大正]]5年（[[1916年]]）、県社に列格した。 社名及び周辺の地名である『警固』はかつて[[鴻臚館]]にあった役所『警固所』に由来する。 ==施設== [[File:Haiden of Kego Shrine 2.jpg|thumb|240px|拝殿]] [[File:Shimmon Gate of Kego Shrine.JPG|thumb|240px|神門]] *拝殿 *本殿 *大鳥居 *神門 *針乃碑 *今益稲荷神社 *神徳殿（貸し会場） [[警固公園]]は神社北隣に接する。 ==交通== *[[西日本鉄道]][[西鉄天神大牟田線|天神大牟田線]][[西鉄福岡（天神）駅]] （南口）徒歩1分 *[[福岡市交通局|福岡市地下鉄]][[福岡市地下鉄空港線|空港線]][[天神駅]] 徒歩7分 *福岡市地下鉄[[福岡市地下鉄七隈線|七隈線]][[天神南駅]] 徒歩4分 *[[西鉄バス]]「天神警固神社・[[三越]]前」 徒歩1分 == 写真 == File:Stone monument at Kego-jinja.JPG File:Stone animal monument at Kego-jinja.jpg ==外部リンク== {{Commonscat|Kego-jinja}} *[http://www.kegojinja.or.jp/ 警固神社]（公式サイト） {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:けこしんしや}} [[Category:福岡県の旧県社]] [[Category:福岡市の神社]] [[Category:福岡市中央区の歴史]] [[Category:福岡市中央区の建築物]] [[Category:福岡天神]] [[Category:神功皇后を祭神とした神社]]

{{Shrine |Name = Kego Shrine (警固神社) |Image = [[File:Torii of Kego Shrine.JPG|250px|{{ill|Kego Shrine Approach|de|Kego Shrine Approach|ja|警固神社参道}}]]
Torii and Worship Hall |Location = {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|de|Fukuoka-Präfektur|ja|福岡県}} {{ill|Fukuoka City|de|Fukuoka-Stadt|ja|福岡市}} {{ill|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|de|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|ja|福岡市中央区 (福岡市)|lt=Chuo Ward|lt_ja=中央区|lt_de=Fukuoka City Chuo Ward}} Tenjin 2-2-20 |Latitude degrees = 33 | Latitude minutes = 35 | Latitude seconds = 16 | N (North Latitude) = N |Longitude degrees = 130 | Longitude minutes = 23 | Longitude seconds = 59.7 | E (East Longitude) = E |Map Country Code = JP |Map = Fukuoka City |Enshrined Deities = Kego Ōkami ({{ill|Kannaobi no Kami|de|Kannaobi no Kami|ja|神直毘神}} ・ {{ill|Kannaobi no Kami|de|Kannaobi no Kami|ja|直毘神|lt=Great Kannaobi no Kami|lt_ja=大直毘神|lt_de=Kannaobi no Kami}} ・ {{ill|Yasomagatsuhinokami|de|Yasomagatsuhinokami|ja|禍津日神|lt=Yasomagatsuhinokami|lt_ja=八十禍津日神|lt_de=Yasomagatsuhinokami}}) |Founded = {{ill|Emperor Chūai|de|Kaiser Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} 9th year |Status = Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|de|Präfektur-Schrein|ja|県社}} |Festival = Saturdays and Sundays after {{ill|July 19|de|19. Juli|ja|7月19日}} (Natsugoshi Festival) }} {{osm box|n|384542332}} '''Kego Shrine''' (けごじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|de|Fukuoka-Präfektur|ja|福岡県}} {{ill|Fukuoka City|de|Fukuoka-Stadt|ja|福岡市}} {{ill|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|de|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|ja|福岡市中央区 (福岡市)|lt=Chuo Ward|lt_ja=中央区|lt_de=Fukuoka City Chuo Ward}} located at Tenjin 2-2-20, and is a {{ill|Shrine|de|Schrein|ja|神社}}. Its former shrine rank was {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|de|Präfektur-Schrein|ja|県社}}. ==Enshrined Deities== Kego Ōkami (the three deities): * {{ill|Yasomagatsuhinokami|de|Yasomagatsuhinokami|ja|禍津日神|lt=Yasomagatsuhinokami|lt_ja=八十禍津日神|lt_de=Yasomagatsuhinokami}} (yasomagatsuhinokami) * {{ill|Kannaobi no Kami|de|Kannaobi no Kami|ja|直毘神|lt=Kannaobi no Kami|lt_ja=神直毘神|lt_de=Kannaobi no Kami}} (kannaobi no kami) * {{ill|Kannaobi no Kami|de|Kannaobi no Kami|ja|直毘神|lt=Great Kannaobi no Kami|lt_ja=大直毘神|lt_de=Great Kannaobi no Kami}} (great kannaobi no kami) ==History== According to shrine legend, during the {{ill|Conquest of the Three Han|de|Eroberung der Drei Han|ja|三韓征伐}}, Empress Jingū’s fleet was protected and led to victory by Kego Ōkami, who was then enshrined at Fukasaki (around the area of the main bailey of {{ill|Fukuoka Castle|de|Fukuoka Schloss|ja|福岡城}}), marking the beginning of the shrine. In the 6th year of the {{ill|Keichō era|de|Keichō-Zeit|ja|慶長}} ({{ill|1601|de|1601|ja|1601年}}), during the construction of Fukuoka Castle, the shrine was moved to Shimokego Village (in the area around present-day {{ill|Fukuoka City|de|Fukuoka-Stadt|ja|福岡市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|de|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|ja|福岡市中央区 (福岡市)|lt=Chuo Ward|lt_ja=中央区|lt_de=Fukuoka City Chuo Ward}}, {{ill|Tenjin|de|Tenjin|ja|天神 (福岡市)|lt=Tenjin|lt_ja=天神|lt_de=Tenjin}})). In the 13th year of the {{ill|Keichō era|de|Keichō-Zeit|ja|慶長}} ({{ill|1608|de|1608|ja|1608年}}), {{ill|Kuroda Nagamasa|de|Kuroda Nagamasa|ja|黒田長政}}, the lord of Fukuoka Castle, had a shrine building erected at its current location. In the 5th year of the {{ill|Taishō era|de|Taishō-Zeit|ja|大正}} ({{ill|1916|de|1916|ja|1916年}}), it was ranked as a Prefectural Shrine. The shrine’s name and the surrounding area “Kego” derive from the former government office “Kegosho” that was once located in the {{ill|Kōrokukan|de|Kōrokukan|ja|鴻臚館}}. ==Facilities== [[File:Haiden of Kego Shrine 2.jpg|thumb|240px|{{ill|Haiden|de|Haiden|ja|拝殿}}]] [[File:Shimmon Gate of Kego Shrine.JPG|thumb|240px|{{ill|Shinmon Gate|de|Shinmon Gate|ja|神門}}]] * Haiden (Worship Hall) * Honden (Main Hall) * Great Torii * Shinmon Gate * Harinoki Monument * Ima Masu Inari Shrine * Shintoku Hall (rental venue) {{ill|Kego Park|de|Kego Park|ja|警固公園}} borders the northern side of the shrine. ==Access== * {{ill|Nishi-Nippon Railroad|de|Nishi-Nippon Railroad|ja|西日本鉄道}} {{ill|Tenjin Omuta Line|de|Tenjin Omuta Line|ja|西鉄天神大牟田線|lt=Tenjin Omuta Line|lt_ja=天神大牟田線|lt_de=Tenjin Omuta Line}} {{ill|Nishitetsu Fukuoka (Tenjin) Station|de|Nishitetsu Fukuoka (Tenjin) Station|ja|西鉄福岡（天神）駅}} (South Exit) 1-minute walk * {{ill|Fukuoka City Subway|de|Fukuoka City Subway|ja|福岡市交通局|lt=Fukuoka City Subway|lt_ja=福岡市地下鉄|lt_de=Fukuoka City Subway}} {{ill|Airport Line|de|Airport Line|ja|福岡市地下鉄空港線|lt=Airport Line|lt_ja=空港線|lt_de=Airport Line}} {{ill|Tenjin Station|de|Tenjin Station|ja|天神駅}} 7-minute walk * Fukuoka City Subway {{ill|Fukuoka City Subway Nanakuma Line|de|Fukuoka City Subway Nanakuma Line|ja|福岡市地下鉄七隈線|lt=Nanakuma Line|lt_ja=七隈線|lt_de=Fukuoka City Subway Nanakuma Line}} {{ill|Tenjin-Minami Station|de|Tenjin-Minami Station|ja|天神南駅}} 4-minute walk * {{ill|Nishitetsu Bus|de|Nishitetsu Bus|ja|西鉄バス}} “Tenjin Kego Shrine – {{ill|Mitsukoshi|de|Mitsukoshi|ja|三越}} Front” 1-minute walk ==Photographs== File:Stone monument at Kego-jinja.JPG File:Stone animal monument at Kego-jinja.jpg ==External Links== {{Commonscat|Kego-jinja}} *[http://www.kegojinja.or.jp/ Kego Shrine] {{Shrine |Name = Kego Shrine (警固神社) |Image = [[File:Torii of Kego Shrine.JPG|250px|{{ill|Kego Shrine Approach|de|Kego Shrine Approach|ja|警固神社参道}}]]
Torii and Worship Hall |Location = {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|de|Fukuoka-Präfektur|ja|福岡県}} {{ill|Fukuoka City|de|Fukuoka-Stadt|ja|福岡市}} {{ill|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|de|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|ja|福岡市中央区 (福岡市)|lt=Chuo Ward|lt_ja=中央区|lt_de=Fukuoka City Chuo Ward}} Tenjin 2-2-20 |Latitude degrees = 33 | Latitude minutes = 35 | Latitude seconds = 16 | N (North Latitude) = N |Longitude degrees = 130 | Longitude minutes = 23 | Longitude seconds = 59.7 | E (East Longitude) = E |Map Country Code = JP |Map = Fukuoka City |Enshrined Deities = Kego Ōkami ({{ill|Kannaobi no Kami|de|Kannaobi no Kami|ja|神直毘神}} ・ {{ill|Kannaobi no Kami|de|Kannaobi no Kami|ja|直毘神|lt=Great Kannaobi no Kami|lt_ja=大直毘神|lt_de=Kannaobi no Kami}} ・ {{ill|Yasomagatsuhinokami|de|Yasomagatsuhinokami|ja|禍津日神|lt=Yasomagatsuhinokami|lt_ja=八十禍津日神|lt_de=Yasomagatsuhinokami}}) |Founded = {{ill|Emperor Chūai|de|Kaiser Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} 9th year |Status = Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|de|Präfektur-Schrein|ja|県社}} |Festival = Saturdays and Sundays after {{ill|July 19|de|19. Juli|ja|7月19日}} (Natsugoshi Festival) }} {{osm box|n|384542332}} '''Kego Shrine''' (けごじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|de|Fukuoka-Präfektur|ja|福岡県}} {{ill|Fukuoka City|de|Fukuoka-Stadt|ja|福岡市}} {{ill|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|de|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|ja|福岡市中央区 (福岡市)|lt=Chuo Ward|lt_ja=中央区|lt_de=Fukuoka City Chuo Ward}} located at Tenjin 2-2-20, and is a {{ill|Shrine|de|Schrein|ja|神社}}. Its former shrine rank was {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|de|Präfektur-Schrein|ja|県社}}. ==Enshrined Deities== Kego Ōkami (the three deities): * {{ill|Yasomagatsuhinokami|de|Yasomagatsuhinokami|ja|禍津日神|lt=Yasomagatsuhinokami|lt_ja=八十禍津日神|lt_de=Yasomagatsuhinokami}} (yasomagatsuhinokami) * {{ill|Kannaobi no Kami|de|Kannaobi no Kami|ja|直毘神|lt=Kannaobi no Kami|lt_ja=神直毘神|lt_de=Kannaobi no Kami}} (kannaobi no kami) * {{ill|Kannaobi no Kami|de|Kannaobi no Kami|ja|直毘神|lt=Great Kannaobi no Kami|lt_ja=大直毘神|lt_de=Great Kannaobi no Kami}} (great kannaobi no kami) ==History== According to shrine legend, during the {{ill|Conquest of the Three Han|de|Eroberung der Drei Han|ja|三韓征伐}}, Empress Jingū’s fleet was protected and led to victory by Kego Ōkami, who was then enshrined at Fukasaki (around the area of the main bailey of {{ill|Fukuoka Castle|de|Fukuoka Schloss|ja|福岡城}}), marking the beginning of the shrine. In the 6th year of the {{ill|Keichō era|de|Keichō-Zeit|ja|慶長}} ({{ill|1601|de|1601|ja|1601年}}), during the construction of Fukuoka Castle, the shrine was moved to Shimokego Village (in the area around present-day {{ill|Fukuoka City|de|Fukuoka-Stadt|ja|福岡市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|de|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|ja|福岡市中央区 (福岡市)|lt=Chuo Ward|lt_ja=中央区|lt_de=Fukuoka City Chuo Ward}}, {{ill|Tenjin|de|Tenjin|ja|天神 (福岡市)|lt=Tenjin|lt_ja=天神|lt_de=Tenjin}})). In the 13th year of the {{ill|Keichō era|de|Keichō-Zeit|ja|慶長}} ({{ill|1608|de|1608|ja|1608年}}), {{ill|Kuroda Nagamasa|de|Kuroda Nagamasa|ja|黒田長政}}, the lord of Fukuoka Castle, had a shrine building erected at its current location. In the 5th year of the {{ill|Taishō era|de|Taishō-Zeit|ja|大正}} ({{ill|1916|de|1916|ja|1916年}}), it was ranked as a Prefectural Shrine. The shrine’s name and the surrounding area “Kego” derive from the former government office “Kegosho” that was once located in the {{ill|Kōrokukan|de|Kōrokukan|ja|鴻臚館}}. ==Facilities== [[File:Haiden of Kego Shrine 2.jpg|thumb|240px|{{ill|Haiden|de|Haiden|ja|拝殿}}]] [[File:Shimmon Gate of Kego Shrine.JPG|thumb|240px|{{ill|Shinmon Gate|de|Shinmon Gate|ja|神門}}]] * Haiden (Worship Hall) * Honden (Main Hall) * Great Torii * Shinmon Gate * Harinoki Monument * Ima Masu Inari Shrine * Shintoku Hall (rental venue) {{ill|Kego Park|de|Kego Park|ja|警固公園}} borders the northern side of the shrine. ==Access== * {{ill|Nishi-Nippon Railroad|de|Nishi-Nippon Railroad|ja|西日本鉄道}} {{ill|Tenjin Omuta Line|de|Tenjin Omuta Line|ja|西鉄天神大牟田線|lt=Tenjin Omuta Line|lt_ja=天神大牟田線|lt_de=Tenjin Omuta Line}} {{ill|Nishitetsu Fukuoka (Tenjin) Station|de|Nishitetsu Fukuoka (Tenjin) Station|ja|西鉄福岡（天神）駅}} (South Exit) 1-minute walk * {{ill|Fukuoka City Subway|de|Fukuoka City Subway|ja|福岡市交通局|lt=Fukuoka City Subway|lt_ja=福岡市地下鉄|lt_de=Fukuoka City Subway}} {{ill|Airport Line|de|Airport Line|ja|福岡市地下鉄空港線|lt=Airport Line|lt_ja=空港線|lt_de=Airport Line}} {{ill|Tenjin Station|de|Tenjin Station|ja|天神駅}} 7-minute walk * Fukuoka City Subway {{ill|Fukuoka City Subway Nanakuma Line|de|Fukuoka City Subway Nanakuma Line|ja|福岡市地下鉄七隈線|lt=Nanakuma Line|lt_ja=七隈線|lt_de=Fukuoka City Subway Nanakuma Line}} {{ill|Tenjin-Minami Station|de|Tenjin-Minami Station|ja|天神南駅}} 4-minute walk * {{ill|Nishitetsu Bus|de|Nishitetsu Bus|ja|西鉄バス}} “Tenjin Kego Shrine – {{ill|Mitsukoshi|de|Mitsukoshi|ja|三越}} Front” 1-minute walk ==Photographs== File:Stone monument at Kego-jinja.JPG File:Stone animal monument at Kego-jinja.jpg ==External Links== {{Commonscat|Kego-jinja}} *[http://www.kegojinja.or.jp/ Kego Shrine] (official website) {{Shinto Side}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines of Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrines in Fukuoka City]] [[Category:History of Chuo Ward, Fukuoka City]] Torii and Worship Hall |Location = {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|de|Fukuoka-Präfektur|ja|福岡県|12=draft}} {{ill|Fukuoka City|de|Fukuoka-Stadt|ja|福岡市|12=draft}} {{ill|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|de|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|ja|福岡市中央区 (福岡市)|lt=Chuo Ward|lt_ja=中央区|lt_de=Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|12=draft}} Tenjin 2-2-20 |Latitude degrees = 33 | Latitude minutes = 35 | Latitude seconds = 16 | N (North Latitude) = N |Longitude degrees = 130 | Longitude minutes = 23 | Longitude seconds = 59.7 | E (East Longitude) = E |Map Country Code = JP |Map = Fukuoka City |Enshrined Deities = Kego Ōkami ({{ill|Kannaobi no Kami|de|Kannaobi no Kami|ja|神直毘神|12=draft}} ・ {{ill|Kannaobi no Kami|de|Kannaobi no Kami|ja|直毘神|lt=Great Kannaobi no Kami|lt_ja=大直毘神|lt_de=Kannaobi no Kami|12=draft}} ・ {{ill|Yasomagatsuhinokami|de|Yasomagatsuhinokami|ja|禍津日神|lt=Yasomagatsuhinokami|lt_ja=八十禍津日神|lt_de=Yasomagatsuhinokami|12=draft}}) |Founded = {{ill|Emperor Chūai|de|Kaiser Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|12=draft}} 9th year |Status = Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|de|Präfektur-Schrein|ja|県社|12=draft}} |Festival = Saturdays and Sundays after {{ill|July 19|de|19. Juli|ja|7月19日|12=draft}} (Natsugoshi Festival) }} {{osm box|n|384542332}} '''Kego Shrine''' (けごじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|de|Fukuoka-Präfektur|ja|福岡県|12=draft}} {{ill|Fukuoka City|de|Fukuoka-Stadt|ja|福岡市|12=draft}} {{ill|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|de|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|ja|福岡市中央区 (福岡市)|lt=Chuo Ward|lt_ja=中央区|lt_de=Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|12=draft}} located at Tenjin 2-2-20, and is a {{ill|Shrine|de|Schrein|ja|神社|12=draft}}. Its former shrine rank was {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|de|Präfektur-Schrein|ja|県社|12=draft}}. ==Enshrined Deities== Kego Ōkami (the three deities): * {{ill|Yasomagatsuhinokami|de|Yasomagatsuhinokami|ja|禍津日神|lt=Yasomagatsuhinokami|lt_ja=八十禍津日神|lt_de=Yasomagatsuhinokami|12=draft}} (yasomagatsuhinokami) * {{ill|Kannaobi no Kami|de|Kannaobi no Kami|ja|直毘神|lt=Kannaobi no Kami|lt_ja=神直毘神|lt_de=Kannaobi no Kami|12=draft}} (kannaobi no kami) * {{ill|Kannaobi no Kami|de|Kannaobi no Kami|ja|直毘神|lt=Great Kannaobi no Kami|lt_ja=大直毘神|lt_de=Great Kannaobi no Kami|12=draft}} (great kannaobi no kami) ==History== According to shrine legend, during the {{ill|Conquest of the Three Han|de|Eroberung der Drei Han|ja|三韓征伐|12=draft}}, Empress Jingū’s fleet was protected and led to victory by Kego Ōkami, who was then enshrined at Fukasaki (around the area of the main bailey of {{ill|Fukuoka Castle|de|Fukuoka Schloss|ja|福岡城|12=draft}}), marking the beginning of the shrine. In the 6th year of the {{ill|Keichō era|de|Keichō-Zeit|ja|慶長|12=draft}} ({{ill|1601|de|1601|ja|1601年|12=draft}}), during the construction of Fukuoka Castle, the shrine was moved to Shimokego Village (in the area around present-day {{ill|Fukuoka City|de|Fukuoka-Stadt|ja|福岡市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|de|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|ja|福岡市中央区 (福岡市)|lt=Chuo Ward|lt_ja=中央区|lt_de=Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tenjin|de|Tenjin|ja|天神 (福岡市)|lt=Tenjin|lt_ja=天神|lt_de=Tenjin|12=draft}})). In the 13th year of the {{ill|Keichō era|de|Keichō-Zeit|ja|慶長|12=draft}} ({{ill|1608|de|1608|ja|1608年|12=draft}}), {{ill|Kuroda Nagamasa|de|Kuroda Nagamasa|ja|黒田長政|12=draft}}, the lord of Fukuoka Castle, had a shrine building erected at its current location. In the 5th year of the {{ill|Taishō era|de|Taishō-Zeit|ja|大正|12=draft}} ({{ill|1916|de|1916|ja|1916年|12=draft}}), it was ranked as a Prefectural Shrine. The shrine’s name and the surrounding area “Kego” derive from the former government office “Kegosho” that was once located in the {{ill|Kōrokukan|de|Kōrokukan|ja|鴻臚館|12=draft}}. ==Facilities== [[File:Haiden of Kego Shrine 2.jpg|thumb|240px|{{ill|Haiden|de|Haiden|ja|拝殿|12=draft}}]] [[File:Shimmon Gate of Kego Shrine.JPG|thumb|240px|{{ill|Shinmon Gate|de|Shinmon Gate|ja|神門|12=draft}}]] * Haiden (Worship Hall) * Honden (Main Hall) * Great Torii * Shinmon Gate * Harinoki Monument * Ima Masu Inari Shrine * Shintoku Hall (rental venue) {{ill|Kego Park|de|Kego Park|ja|警固公園|12=draft}} borders the northern side of the shrine. ==Access== * {{ill|Nishi-Nippon Railroad|de|Nishi-Nippon Railroad|ja|西日本鉄道|12=draft}} {{ill|Tenjin Omuta Line|de|Tenjin Omuta Line|ja|西鉄天神大牟田線|lt=Tenjin Omuta Line|lt_ja=天神大牟田線|lt_de=Tenjin Omuta Line|12=draft}} {{ill|Nishitetsu Fukuoka (Tenjin) Station|de|Nishitetsu Fukuoka (Tenjin) Station|ja|西鉄福岡（天神）駅|12=draft}} (South Exit) 1-minute walk * {{ill|Fukuoka City Subway|de|Fukuoka City Subway|ja|福岡市交通局|lt=Fukuoka City Subway|lt_ja=福岡市地下鉄|lt_de=Fukuoka City Subway|12=draft}} {{ill|Airport Line|de|Airport Line|ja|福岡市地下鉄空港線|lt=Airport Line|lt_ja=空港線|lt_de=Airport Line|12=draft}} {{ill|Tenjin Station|de|Tenjin Station|ja|天神駅|12=draft}} 7-minute walk * Fukuoka City Subway {{ill|Fukuoka City Subway Nanakuma Line|de|Fukuoka City Subway Nanakuma Line|ja|福岡市地下鉄七隈線|lt=Nanakuma Line|lt_ja=七隈線|lt_de=Fukuoka City Subway Nanakuma Line|12=draft}} {{ill|Tenjin-Minami Station|de|Tenjin-Minami Station|ja|天神南駅|12=draft}} 4-minute walk * {{ill|Nishitetsu Bus|de|Nishitetsu Bus|ja|西鉄バス|12=draft}} “Tenjin Kego Shrine – {{ill|Mitsukoshi|de|Mitsukoshi|ja|三越|12=draft}} Front” 1-minute walk ==Photographs== File:Stone monument at {{Shrine |Name = Kego Shrine (警固神社) |image = [[File:Torii of Kego Shrine.JPG|250px|{{ill|Kego Shrine Approach|de|Kego Shrine Approach|ja|警固神社参道|12=draft}}]]
Torii and Worship Hall |Location = {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|de|Fukuoka-Präfektur|ja|福岡県|12=draft}} {{ill|Fukuoka City|de|Fukuoka-Stadt|ja|福岡市|12=draft}} {{ill|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|de|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|ja|福岡市中央区 (福岡市)|lt=Chuo Ward|lt_ja=中央区|lt_de=Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|12=draft}} Tenjin 2-2-20 |Latitude degrees = 33 | Latitude minutes = 35 | Latitude seconds = 16 | N (North Latitude) = N |Longitude degrees = 130 | Longitude minutes = 23 | Longitude seconds = 59.7 | E (East Longitude) = E |Map Country Code = JP |Map = Fukuoka City |Enshrined Deities = Kego Ōkami ({{ill|Kannaobi no Kami|de|Kannaobi no Kami|ja|神直毘神|12=draft}} ・ {{ill|Kannaobi no Kami|de|Kannaobi no Kami|ja|直毘神|lt=Great Kannaobi no Kami|lt_ja=大直毘神|lt_de=Kannaobi no Kami|12=draft}} ・ {{ill|Yasomagatsuhinokami|de|Yasomagatsuhinokami|ja|禍津日神|lt=Yasomagatsuhinokami|lt_ja=八十禍津日神|lt_de=Yasomagatsuhinokami|12=draft}}) |Founded = {{ill|Emperor Chūai|de|Kaiser Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|12=draft}} 9th year |Status = Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|de|Präfektur-Schrein|ja|県社|12=draft}} |Festival = Saturdays and Sundays after {{ill|July 19|de|19. Juli|ja|7月19日|12=draft}} (Natsugoshi Festival) }} {{osm box|n|384542332}} '''Kego Shrine''' (けごじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|de|Fukuoka-Präfektur|ja|福岡県|12=draft}} {{ill|Fukuoka City|de|Fukuoka-Stadt|ja|福岡市|12=draft}} {{ill|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|de|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|ja|福岡市中央区 (福岡市)|lt=Chuo Ward|lt_ja=中央区|lt_de=Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|12=draft}} located at Tenjin 2-2-20, and is a {{ill|Shrine|de|Schrein|ja|神社|12=draft}}. Its former shrine rank was {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|de|Präfektur-Schrein|ja|県社|12=draft}}. ==Enshrined Deities== Kego Ōkami (the three deities): * {{ill|Yasomagatsuhinokami|de|Yasomagatsuhinokami|ja|禍津日神|lt=Yasomagatsuhinokami|lt_ja=八十禍津日神|lt_de=Yasomagatsuhinokami|12=draft}} (yasomagatsuhinokami) * {{ill|Kannaobi no Kami|de|Kannaobi no Kami|ja|直毘神|lt=Kannaobi no Kami|lt_ja=神直毘神|lt_de=Kannaobi no Kami|12=draft}} (kannaobi no kami) * {{ill|Kannaobi no Kami|de|Kannaobi no Kami|ja|直毘神|lt=Great Kannaobi no Kami|lt_ja=大直毘神|lt_de=Great Kannaobi no Kami|12=draft}} (great kannaobi no kami) ==History== According to shrine legend, during the {{ill|Conquest of the Three Han|de|Eroberung der Drei Han|ja|三韓征伐|12=draft}}, Empress Jingū’s fleet was protected and led to victory by Kego Ōkami, who was then enshrined at Fukasaki (around the area of the main bailey of {{ill|Fukuoka Castle|de|Fukuoka Schloss|ja|福岡城|12=draft}}), marking the beginning of the shrine. In the 6th year of the {{ill|Keichō era|de|Keichō-Zeit|ja|慶長|12=draft}} ({{ill|1601|de|1601|ja|1601年|12=draft}}), during the construction of Fukuoka Castle, the shrine was moved to Shimokego Village (in the area around present-day {{ill|Fukuoka City|de|Fukuoka-Stadt|ja|福岡市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|de|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|ja|福岡市中央区 (福岡市)|lt=Chuo Ward|lt_ja=中央区|lt_de=Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tenjin|de|Tenjin|ja|天神 (福岡市)|lt=Tenjin|lt_ja=天神|lt_de=Tenjin|12=draft}})). In the 13th year of the {{ill|Keichō era|de|Keichō-Zeit|ja|慶長|12=draft}} ({{ill|1608|de|1608|ja|1608年|12=draft}}), {{ill|Kuroda Nagamasa|de|Kuroda Nagamasa|ja|黒田長政|12=draft}}, the lord of Fukuoka Castle, had a shrine building erected at its current location. In the 5th year of the {{ill|Taishō era|de|Taishō-Zeit|ja|大正|12=draft}} ({{ill|1916|de|1916|ja|1916年|12=draft}}), it was ranked as a Prefectural Shrine. The shrine’s name and the surrounding area “Kego” derive from the former government office “Kegosho” that was once located in the {{ill|Kōrokukan|de|Kōrokukan|ja|鴻臚館|12=draft}}. ==Facilities== [[File:Haiden of Kego Shrine 2.jpg|thumb|240px|{{ill|Haiden|de|Haiden|ja|拝殿|12=draft}}]] [[File:Shimmon Gate of Kego Shrine.JPG|thumb|240px|{{ill|Shinmon Gate|de|Shinmon Gate|ja|神門|12=draft}}]] * Haiden (Worship Hall) * Honden (Main Hall) * Great Torii * Shinmon Gate * Harinoki Monument * Ima Masu Inari Shrine * Shintoku Hall (rental venue) {{ill|Kego Park|de|Kego Park|ja|警固公園|12=draft}} borders the northern side of the shrine. ==Access== * {{ill|Nishi-Nippon Railroad|de|Nishi-Nippon Railroad|ja|西日本鉄道|12=draft}} {{ill|Tenjin Omuta Line|de|Tenjin Omuta Line|ja|西鉄天神大牟田線|lt=Tenjin Omuta Line|lt_ja=天神大牟田線|lt_de=Tenjin Omuta Line|12=draft}} {{ill|Nishitetsu Fukuoka (Tenjin) Station|de|Nishitetsu Fukuoka (Tenjin) Station|ja|西鉄福岡（天神）駅|12=draft}} (South Exit) 1-minute walk * {{ill|Fukuoka City Subway|de|Fukuoka City Subway|ja|福岡市交通局|lt=Fukuoka City Subway|lt_ja=福岡市地下鉄|lt_de=Fukuoka City Subway|12=draft}} {{ill|Airport Line|de|Airport Line|ja|福岡市地下鉄空港線|lt=Airport Line|lt_ja=空港線|lt_de=Airport Line|12=draft}} {{ill|Tenjin Station|de|Tenjin Station|ja|天神駅|12=draft}} 7-minute walk * Fukuoka City Subway {{ill|Fukuoka City Subway Nanakuma Line|de|Fukuoka City Subway Nanakuma Line|ja|福岡市地下鉄七隈線|lt=Nanakuma Line|lt_ja=七隈線|lt_de=Fukuoka City Subway Nanakuma Line|12=draft}} {{ill|Tenjin-Minami Station|de|Tenjin-Minami Station|ja|天神南駅|12=draft}} 4-minute walk * {{ill|Nishitetsu Bus|de|Nishitetsu Bus|ja|西鉄バス|12=draft}} “Tenjin Kego Shrine – {{ill|Mitsukoshi|de|Mitsukoshi|ja|三越|12=draft}} Front” 1-minute walk ==Photographs== File:Stone monument at Kego-jinja.JPG File:Stone animal monument at Kego-jinja.jpg ==External Links== {{Commonscat|Kego-jinja}} *[http://www.kegojinja.or.jp/ Kego Shrine] (official website) {{Shinto Side}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines of Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrines in Fukuoka City]] [[Category:History of Chuo Ward, Fukuoka City]] Kego-jinja.JPG File:Stone animal monument at Kego-jinja.jpg ==External Links== {{Commonscat|Kego-jinja}} *[http://www.kegojinja.or.jp/ Kego Shrine] (official website) {{Shinto Side}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Buildings in Chuo Ward, Fukuoka City]] [[Category:Fukuoka Tenjin]] [[Category:Shrines enshrining Empress Jingū]] [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:警固神社]] [[de:Kego {{Shrine |Name = Kego Shrine (警固神社) |image = [[File:Torii of Kego Shrine.JPG|250px|{{ill|Kego Shrine Approach|de|Kego Shrine Approach|ja|警固神社参道|12=draft}}]]
Torii and Worship Hall |Location = {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|de|Fukuoka-Präfektur|ja|福岡県|12=draft}} {{ill|Fukuoka City|de|Fukuoka-Stadt|ja|福岡市|12=draft}} {{ill|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|de|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|ja|福岡市中央区 (福岡市)|lt=Chuo Ward|lt_ja=中央区|lt_de=Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|12=draft}} Tenjin 2-2-20 |Latitude degrees = 33 | Latitude minutes = 35 | Latitude seconds = 16 | N (North Latitude) = N |Longitude degrees = 130 | Longitude minutes = 23 | Longitude seconds = 59.7 | E (East Longitude) = E |Map Country Code = JP |Map = Fukuoka City |Enshrined Deities = Kego Ōkami ({{ill|Kannaobi no Kami|de|Kannaobi no Kami|ja|神直毘神|12=draft}} ・ {{ill|Kannaobi no Kami|de|Kannaobi no Kami|ja|直毘神|lt=Great Kannaobi no Kami|lt_ja=大直毘神|lt_de=Kannaobi no Kami|12=draft}} ・ {{ill|Yasomagatsuhinokami|de|Yasomagatsuhinokami|ja|禍津日神|lt=Yasomagatsuhinokami|lt_ja=八十禍津日神|lt_de=Yasomagatsuhinokami|12=draft}}) |Founded = {{ill|Emperor Chūai|de|Kaiser Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|12=draft}} 9th year |Status = Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|de|Präfektur-Schrein|ja|県社|12=draft}} |Festival = Saturdays and Sundays after {{ill|July 19|de|19. Juli|ja|7月19日|12=draft}} (Natsugoshi Festival) }} {{osm box|n|384542332}} '''Kego Shrine''' (けごじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|de|Fukuoka-Präfektur|ja|福岡県|12=draft}} {{ill|Fukuoka City|de|Fukuoka-Stadt|ja|福岡市|12=draft}} {{ill|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|de|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|ja|福岡市中央区 (福岡市)|lt=Chuo Ward|lt_ja=中央区|lt_de=Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|12=draft}} located at Tenjin 2-2-20, and is a {{ill|Shrine|de|Schrein|ja|神社|12=draft}}. Its former shrine rank was {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|de|Präfektur-Schrein|ja|県社|12=draft}}. ==Enshrined Deities== Kego Ōkami (the three deities): * {{ill|Yasomagatsuhinokami|de|Yasomagatsuhinokami|ja|禍津日神|lt=Yasomagatsuhinokami|lt_ja=八十禍津日神|lt_de=Yasomagatsuhinokami|12=draft}} (yasomagatsuhinokami) * {{ill|Kannaobi no Kami|de|Kannaobi no Kami|ja|直毘神|lt=Kannaobi no Kami|lt_ja=神直毘神|lt_de=Kannaobi no Kami|12=draft}} (kannaobi no kami) * {{ill|Kannaobi no Kami|de|Kannaobi no Kami|ja|直毘神|lt=Great Kannaobi no Kami|lt_ja=大直毘神|lt_de=Great Kannaobi no Kami|12=draft}} (great kannaobi no kami) ==History== According to shrine legend, during the {{ill|Conquest of the Three Han|de|Eroberung der Drei Han|ja|三韓征伐|12=draft}}, Empress Jingū’s fleet was protected and led to victory by Kego Ōkami, who was then enshrined at Fukasaki (around the area of the main bailey of {{ill|Fukuoka Castle|de|Fukuoka Schloss|ja|福岡城|12=draft}}), marking the beginning of the shrine. In the 6th year of the {{ill|Keichō era|de|Keichō-Zeit|ja|慶長|12=draft}} ({{ill|1601|de|1601|ja|1601年|12=draft}}), during the construction of Fukuoka Castle, the shrine was moved to Shimokego Village (in the area around present-day {{ill|Fukuoka City|de|Fukuoka-Stadt|ja|福岡市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|de|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|ja|福岡市中央区 (福岡市)|lt=Chuo Ward|lt_ja=中央区|lt_de=Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tenjin|de|Tenjin|ja|天神 (福岡市)|lt=Tenjin|lt_ja=天神|lt_de=Tenjin|12=draft}})). In the 13th year of the {{ill|Keichō era|de|Keichō-Zeit|ja|慶長|12=draft}} ({{ill|1608|de|1608|ja|1608年|12=draft}}), {{ill|Kuroda Nagamasa|de|Kuroda Nagamasa|ja|黒田長政|12=draft}}, the lord of Fukuoka Castle, had a shrine building erected at its current location. In the 5th year of the {{ill|Taishō era|de|Taishō-Zeit|ja|大正|12=draft}} ({{ill|1916|de|1916|ja|1916年|12=draft}}), it was ranked as a Prefectural Shrine. The shrine’s name and the surrounding area “Kego” derive from the former government office “Kegosho” that was once located in the {{ill|Kōrokukan|de|Kōrokukan|ja|鴻臚館|12=draft}}. ==Facilities== [[File:Haiden of Kego Shrine 2.jpg|thumb|240px|{{ill|Haiden|de|Haiden|ja|拝殿|12=draft}}]] [[File:Shimmon Gate of Kego Shrine.JPG|thumb|240px|{{ill|Shinmon Gate|de|Shinmon Gate|ja|神門|12=draft}}]] * Haiden (Worship Hall) * Honden (Main Hall) * Great Torii * Shinmon Gate * Harinoki Monument * Ima Masu Inari Shrine * Shintoku Hall (rental venue) {{ill|Kego Park|de|Kego Park|ja|警固公園|12=draft}} borders the northern side of the shrine. ==Access== * {{ill|Nishi-Nippon Railroad|de|Nishi-Nippon Railroad|ja|西日本鉄道|12=draft}} {{ill|Tenjin Omuta Line|de|Tenjin Omuta Line|ja|西鉄天神大牟田線|lt=Tenjin Omuta Line|lt_ja=天神大牟田線|lt_de=Tenjin Omuta Line|12=draft}} {{ill|Nishitetsu Fukuoka (Tenjin) Station|de|Nishitetsu Fukuoka (Tenjin) Station|ja|西鉄福岡（天神）駅|12=draft}} (South Exit) 1-minute walk * {{ill|Fukuoka City Subway|de|Fukuoka City Subway|ja|福岡市交通局|lt=Fukuoka City Subway|lt_ja=福岡市地下鉄|lt_de=Fukuoka City Subway|12=draft}} {{ill|Airport Line|de|Airport Line|ja|福岡市地下鉄空港線|lt=Airport Line|lt_ja=空港線|lt_de=Airport Line|12=draft}} {{ill|Tenjin Station|de|Tenjin Station|ja|天神駅|12=draft}} 7-minute walk * Fukuoka City Subway {{ill|Fukuoka City Subway Nanakuma Line|de|Fukuoka City Subway Kego-jinja.JPG File:Stone animal monument at Kego-jinja.jpg ==External Links== {{Commonscat|Kego-jinja}} *[http://www.kegojinja.or.jp/ Kego Shrine] (official website) {{Shinto Side}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Shrine |Name = Kego Shrine (警固神社) |image = [[File:Torii of Kego Shrine.JPG|250px|{{ill|Kego Shrine Approach|de|Kego Shrine Approach|ja|警固神社参道}}]]
Torii and Worship Hall |Location = {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|de|Fukuoka-Präfektur|ja|福岡県}} {{ill|Fukuoka City|de|Fukuoka-Stadt|ja|福岡市}} {{ill|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|de|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|ja|福岡市中央区 (福岡市)|lt=Chuo Ward|lt_ja=中央区|lt_de=Fukuoka City Chuo Ward}} Tenjin 2-2-20 |Latitude degrees = 33 | Latitude minutes = 35 | Latitude seconds = 16 | N (North Latitude) = N |Longitude degrees = 130 | Longitude minutes = 23 | Longitude seconds = 59.7 | E (East Longitude) = E |Map Country Code = JP |Map = Fukuoka City |Enshrined Deities = Kego Ōkami ({{ill|Kannaobi no Kami|de|Kannaobi no Kami|ja|神直毘神}} ・ {{ill|Kannaobi no Kami|de|Kannaobi no Kami|ja|直毘神|lt=Great Kannaobi no Kami|lt_ja=大直毘神|lt_de=Kannaobi no Kami}} ・ {{ill|Yasomagatsuhinokami|de|Yasomagatsuhinokami|ja|禍津日神|lt=Yasomagatsuhinokami|lt_ja=八十禍津日神|lt_de=Yasomagatsuhinokami}}) |Founded = {{ill|Emperor Chūai|de|Kaiser Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} 9th year |Status = Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|de|Präfektur-Schrein|ja|県社}} |Festival = Saturdays and Sundays after {{ill|July 19|de|19. Juli|ja|7月19日}} (Natsugoshi Festival) }} {{osm box|n|384542332}} '''Kego Shrine''' (けごじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|de|Fukuoka-Präfektur|ja|福岡県}} {{ill|Fukuoka City|de|Fukuoka-Stadt|ja|福岡市}} {{ill|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|de|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|ja|福岡市中央区 (福岡市)|lt=Chuo Ward|lt_ja=中央区|lt_de=Fukuoka City Chuo Ward}} located at Tenjin 2-2-20, and is a {{ill|Shrine|de|Schrein|ja|神社}}. Its former shrine Rank was {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|de|Präfektur-Schrein|ja|県社}}. ==Enshrined Deities== Kego Ōkami (the three deities): * {{ill|Yasomagatsuhinokami|de|Yasomagatsuhinokami|ja|禍津日神|lt=Yasomagatsuhinokami|lt_ja=八十禍津日神|lt_de=Yasomagatsuhinokami}} (yasomagatsuhinokami) * {{ill|Kannaobi no Kami|de|Kannaobi no Kami|ja|直毘神|lt=Kannaobi no Kami|lt_ja=神直毘神|lt_de=Kannaobi no Kami}} (kannaobi no kami) * {{ill|Kannaobi no Kami|de|Kannaobi no Kami|ja|直毘神|lt=Great Kannaobi no Kami|lt_ja=大直毘神|lt_de=Great Kannaobi no Kami}} (great kannaobi no kami) ==History== According to shrine legend, during the {{ill|Conquest of the Three Han|de|Eroberung der Drei Han|ja|三韓征伐}}, Empress Jingū’s fleet was protected and led to victory by Kego Ōkami, who was then enshrined at Fukasaki (around the area of the main bailey of {{ill|Fukuoka Castle|de|Fukuoka Schloss|ja|福岡城}}), marking the beginning of the shrine. In the 6th year of the {{ill|Keichō era|de|Keichō-Zeit|ja|慶長}} ({{ill|1601|de|1601|ja|1601年}}), during the construction of Fukuoka Castle, the shrine was moved to Shimokego Village (in the area around present-day {{ill|Fukuoka City|de|Fukuoka-Stadt|ja|福岡市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|de|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|ja|福岡市中央区 (福岡市)|lt=Chuo Ward|lt_ja=中央区|lt_de=Fukuoka City Chuo Ward}}, {{ill|Tenjin|de|Tenjin|ja|天神 (福岡市)|lt=Tenjin|lt_ja=天神|lt_de=Tenjin}})). In the 13th year of the {{ill|Keichō era|de|Keichō-Zeit|ja|慶長}} ({{ill|1608|de|1608|ja|1608年}}), {{ill|Kuroda Nagamasa|de|Kuroda Nagamasa|ja|黒田長政}}, the lord of Fukuoka Castle, had a shrine building erected at its current location. In the 5th year of the {{ill|Taishō era|de|Taishō-Zeit|ja|大正}} ({{ill|1916|de|1916|ja|1916年}}), it was Ranked as a Prefectural Shrine. The shrine’s name and the surrounding area “Kego” derive from the former government office “Kegosho” that was once located in the {{ill|Kōrokukan|de|Kōrokukan|ja|鴻臚館}}. ==Facilities== [[File:Haiden of Kego Shrine 2.jpg|thumb|240px|{{ill|Haiden|de|Haiden|ja|拝殿}}]] [[File:Shimmon Gate of Kego Shrine.JPG|thumb|240px|{{ill|Shinmon Gate|de|Shinmon Gate|ja|神門}}]] * Haiden (Worship Hall) * Honden (Main Hall) * Great Torii * Shinmon Gate * Harinoki Monument * Ima Masu Inari Shrine * Shintoku Hall (rental venue) {{ill|Kego Park|de|Kego Park|ja|警固公園}} borders the northern side of the shrine. ==Access== revision.comment: 45 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:警固神社 up to rev 103161857
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称=警固神社 |画像 = [[File:Torii of Kego Shrine.JPG|250px|警固神社参道]]
鳥居と拝殿 |所在地=[[福岡県]][[福岡市]][[中央区 (福岡市)|中央区]]天神2-2-20 | 緯度度 = 33 | 緯度分 = 35 | 緯度秒 = 16 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 130 |経度分 = 23 | 経度秒 = 59.7 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 地図 = Fukuoka city |祭神=警固大神（[[神直毘神]]・[[大直毘神]]・[[八十禍津日神]]） |創建=[[仲哀天皇]]9年 |社格=旧[[県社]] |例祭=[[7月19日]]以降の土曜・日曜日（夏越し祭） }} {{osm box|n|384542332}} '''警固神社'''（けごじんじゃ）は、[[福岡県]][[福岡市]][[中央区 (福岡市)|中央区]]にある[[神社]]である。旧[[社格]]は[[県社]]。 ==祭神== 警固大神（八十禍津日・神神直毘神・大直毘神の三柱） 　[[禍津日神|八十禍津日神]]（やそまがつひのかみ） 　[[直毘神|神直毘神]]（かんなおびのかみ） 　[[直毘神|大直毘神]]（おおなおびのかみ） ==歴史== 社伝では神功皇后による[[三韓征伐]]の際、皇后の船団を守護し勝利に導いた警固大神を福崎（現在の[[福岡城]]本丸周辺）の地に祀ったのが始まりとされる。[[慶長]]6年（[[1601年]]）福岡城築城の際に下警固村（現在の[[福岡市]][[中央区 (福岡市)|中央区]][[天神 (福岡市)|天神]]周辺）に移され、[[慶長]]13年（[[1608年]]）に福岡城主[[黒田長政]]によって現在の場所に社殿が造営された。[[大正]]5年（[[1916年]]）、県社に列格した。 社名及び周辺の地名である『警固』はかつて[[鴻臚館]]にあった役所『警固所』に由来する。 ==施設== [[File:Haiden of Kego Shrine 2.jpg|thumb|240px|拝殿]] [[File:Shimmon Gate of Kego Shrine.JPG|thumb|240px|神門]] *拝殿 *本殿 *大鳥居 *神門 *針乃碑 *今益稲荷神社 *神徳殿（貸し会場） [[警固公園]]は神社北隣に接する。 ==交通== *[[西日本鉄道]][[西鉄天神大牟田線|天神大牟田線]][[西鉄福岡（天神）駅]] （南口）徒歩1分 *[[福岡市交通局|福岡市地下鉄]][[福岡市地下鉄空港線|空港線]][[天神駅]] 徒歩7分 *福岡市地下鉄[[福岡市地下鉄七隈線|七隈線]][[天神南駅]] 徒歩4分 *[[西鉄バス]]「天神警固神社・[[三越]]前」 徒歩1分 == 写真 == File:Stone monument at Kego-jinja.JPG File:Stone animal monument at Kego-jinja.jpg ==外部リンク== {{Commonscat|Kego-jinja}} *[http://www.kegojinja.or.jp/ 警固神社]（公式サイト） {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:けこしんしや}} [[Category:福岡県の旧県社]] [[Category:福岡市の神社]] [[Category:福岡市中央区の歴史]] [[Category:福岡市中央区の建築物]] [[Category:福岡天神]] [[Category:神功皇后を祭神とした神社]]

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称=警固神社 |画像 = [[File:Torii of Kego Shrine.JPG|250px|警固神社参道]]
鳥居と拝殿 |所在地=[[福岡県]][[福岡市]][[中央区 (福岡市)|中央区]]天神2-2-20 | 緯度度 = 33 | 緯度分 = 35 | 緯度秒 = 16 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 130 |経度分 = 23 | 経度秒 = 59.7 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 地図 = Fukuoka city |祭神=警固大神（[[神直毘神]]・[[大直毘神]]・[[八十禍津日神]]） |創建=[[仲哀天皇]]9年 |社格=旧[[県社]] |例祭=[[7月19日]]以降の土曜・日曜日（夏越し祭） }} {{osm box|n|384542332}} '''警固神社'''（けごじんじゃ）は、[[福岡県]][[福岡市]][[中央区 (福岡市)|中央区]]にある[[神社]]である。旧[[社格]]は[[県社]]。 ==祭神== 警固大神（八十禍津日・神神直毘神・大直毘神の三柱） 　[[禍津日神|八十禍津日神]]（やそまがつひのかみ） 　[[直毘神|神直毘神]]（かんなおびのかみ） 　[[直毘神|大直毘神]]（おおなおびのかみ） ==歴史== 社伝では神功皇后による[[三韓征伐]]の際、皇后の船団を守護し勝利に導いた警固大神を福崎（現在の[[福岡城]]本丸周辺）の地に祀ったのが始まりとされる。[[慶長]]6年（[[1601年]]）福岡城築城の際に下警固村（現在の[[福岡市]][[中央区 (福岡市)|中央区]][[天神 (福岡市)|天神]]周辺）に移され、[[慶長]]13年（[[1608年]]）に福岡城主[[黒田長政]]によって現在の場所に社殿が造営された。[[大正]]5年（[[1916年]]）、県社に列格した。 社名及び周辺の地名である『警固』はかつて[[鴻臚館]]にあった役所『警固所』に由来する。 ==施設== [[File:Haiden of Kego Shrine 2.jpg|thumb|240px|拝殿]] [[File:Shimmon Gate of Kego Shrine.JPG|thumb|240px|神門]] *拝殿 *本殿 *大鳥居 *神門 *針乃碑 *今益稲荷神社 *神徳殿（貸し会場） [[警固公園]]は神社北隣に接する。 ==交通== *[[西日本鉄道]][[西鉄天神大牟田線|天神大牟田線]][[西鉄福岡（天神）駅]] （南口）徒歩1分 *[[福岡市交通局|福岡市地下鉄]][[福岡市地下鉄空港線|空港線]][[天神駅]] 徒歩7分 *福岡市地下鉄[[福岡市地下鉄七隈線|七隈線]][[天神南駅]] 徒歩4分 *[[西鉄バス]]「天神警固神社・[[三越]]前」 徒歩1分 == 写真 == File:Stone monument at Kego-jinja.JPG File:Stone animal monument at Kego-jinja.jpg ==外部リンク== {{Commonscat|Kego-jinja}} *[http://www.kegojinja.or.jp/ 警固神社]（公式サイト） {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:けこしんしや}} [[Category:福岡県の旧県社]] [[Category:福岡市の神社]] [[Category:福岡市中央区の歴史]] [[Category:福岡市中央区の建築物]] [[Category:福岡天神]] [[Category:神功皇后を祭神とした神社]]

{{Shrine |Name = Kego Shrine (警固神社) |image = [[File:Torii of Kego Shrine.JPG|250px|{{ill|Kego Shrine Approach|de|Kego Shrine Approach|ja|警固神社参道}}]]
Torii and Worship Hall |Location = {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|de|Fukuoka-Präfektur|ja|福岡県}} {{ill|Fukuoka City|de|Fukuoka-Stadt|ja|福岡市}} {{ill|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|de|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|ja|福岡市中央区 (福岡市)|lt=Chuo Ward|lt_ja=中央区|lt_de=Fukuoka City Chuo Ward}} Tenjin 2-2-20 |Latitude degrees = 33 | Latitude minutes = 35 | Latitude seconds = 16 | N (North Latitude) = N |Longitude degrees = 130 | Longitude minutes = 23 | Longitude seconds = 59.7 | E (East Longitude) = E |Map Country Code = JP |Map = Fukuoka City |Enshrined Deities = Kego Ōkami ({{ill|Kannaobi no Kami|de|Kannaobi no Kami|ja|神直毘神}} ・ {{ill|Kannaobi no Kami|de|Kannaobi no Kami|ja|直毘神|lt=Great Kannaobi no Kami|lt_ja=大直毘神|lt_de=Kannaobi no Kami}} ・ {{ill|Yasomagatsuhinokami|de|Yasomagatsuhinokami|ja|禍津日神|lt=Yasomagatsuhinokami|lt_ja=八十禍津日神|lt_de=Yasomagatsuhinokami}}) |Founded = {{ill|Emperor Chūai|de|Kaiser Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} 9th year |Status = Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|de|Präfektur-Schrein|ja|県社}} |Festival = Saturdays and Sundays after {{ill|July 19|de|19. Juli|ja|7月19日}} (Natsugoshi Festival) }} {{osm box|n|384542332}} '''Kego Shrine''' (けごじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|de|Fukuoka-Präfektur|ja|福岡県}} {{ill|Fukuoka City|de|Fukuoka-Stadt|ja|福岡市}} {{ill|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|de|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|ja|福岡市中央区 (福岡市)|lt=Chuo Ward|lt_ja=中央区|lt_de=Fukuoka City Chuo Ward}} located at Tenjin 2-2-20, and is a {{ill|Shrine|de|Schrein|ja|神社}}. Its former shrine Rank was {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|de|Präfektur-Schrein|ja|県社}}. ==Enshrined Deities== Kego Ōkami (the three deities): * {{ill|Yasomagatsuhinokami|de|Yasomagatsuhinokami|ja|禍津日神|lt=Yasomagatsuhinokami|lt_ja=八十禍津日神|lt_de=Yasomagatsuhinokami}} (yasomagatsuhinokami) * {{ill|Kannaobi no Kami|de|Kannaobi no Kami|ja|直毘神|lt=Kannaobi no Kami|lt_ja=神直毘神|lt_de=Kannaobi no Kami}} (kannaobi no kami) * {{ill|Kannaobi no Kami|de|Kannaobi no Kami|ja|直毘神|lt=Great Kannaobi no Kami|lt_ja=大直毘神|lt_de=Great Kannaobi no Kami}} (great kannaobi no kami) ==History== According to shrine legend, during the {{ill|Conquest of the Three Han|de|Eroberung der Drei Han|ja|三韓征伐}}, Empress Jingū’s fleet was protected and led to victory by Kego Ōkami, who was then enshrined at Fukasaki (around the area of the main bailey of {{ill|Fukuoka Castle|de|Fukuoka Schloss|ja|福岡城}}), marking the beginning of the shrine. In the 6th year of the {{ill|Keichō era|de|Keichō-Zeit|ja|慶長}} ({{ill|1601|de|1601|ja|1601年}}), during the construction of Fukuoka Castle, the shrine was moved to Shimokego Village (in the area around present-day {{ill|Fukuoka City|de|Fukuoka-Stadt|ja|福岡市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|de|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|ja|福岡市中央区 (福岡市)|lt=Chuo Ward|lt_ja=中央区|lt_de=Fukuoka City Chuo Ward}}, {{ill|Tenjin|de|Tenjin|ja|天神 (福岡市)|lt=Tenjin|lt_ja=天神|lt_de=Tenjin}})). In the 13th year of the {{ill|Keichō era|de|Keichō-Zeit|ja|慶長}} ({{ill|1608|de|1608|ja|1608年}}), {{ill|Kuroda Nagamasa|de|Kuroda Nagamasa|ja|黒田長政}}, the lord of Fukuoka Castle, had a shrine building erected at its current location. In the 5th year of the {{ill|Taishō era|de|Taishō-Zeit|ja|大正}} ({{ill|1916|de|1916|ja|1916年}}), it was Ranked as a Prefectural Shrine. The shrine’s name and the surrounding area “Kego” derive from the former government office “Kegosho” that was once located in the {{ill|Kōrokukan|de|Kōrokukan|ja|鴻臚館}}. ==Facilities== [[File:Haiden of Kego Shrine 2.jpg|thumb|240px|{{ill|Haiden|de|Haiden|ja|拝殿}}]] [[File:Shimmon Gate of Kego Shrine.JPG|thumb|240px|{{ill|Shinmon Gate|de|Shinmon Gate|ja|神門}}]] * Haiden (Worship Hall) * Honden (Main Hall) * Great Torii * Shinmon Gate * Harinoki Monument * Ima Masu Inari Shrine * Shintoku Hall (rental venue) {{ill|Kego Park|de|Kego Park|ja|警固公園}} borders the northern side of the shrine. ==Access== * {{ill|Nishi-Nippon Railroad|de|Nishi-Nippon Railroad|ja|西日本鉄道}} {{ill|Tenjin Omuta Line|de|Tenjin Omuta Line|ja|西鉄天神大牟田線|lt=Tenjin Omuta Line|lt_ja=天神大牟田線|lt_de=Tenjin Omuta Line}} {{ill|Nishitetsu Fukuoka (Tenjin) Station|de|Nishitetsu Fukuoka (Tenjin) Station|ja|西鉄福岡（天神）駅}} (South Exit) 1-minute walk * {{ill|Fukuoka City Subway|de|Fukuoka City Subway|ja|福岡市交通局|lt=Fukuoka City Subway|lt_ja=福岡市地下鉄|lt_de=Fukuoka City Subway}} {{ill|Airport Line|de|Airport Line|ja|福岡市地下鉄空港線|lt=Airport Line|lt_ja=空港線|lt_de=Airport Line}} {{ill|Tenjin Station|de|Tenjin Station|ja|天神駅}} 7-minute walk * Fukuoka City Subway {{ill|Fukuoka City Subway Nanakuma Line|de|Fukuoka City Subway Nanakuma Line|ja|福岡市地下鉄七隈線|lt=Nanakuma Line|lt_ja=七隈線|lt_de=Fukuoka City Subway Nanakuma Line}} {{ill|Tenjin-Minami Station|de|Tenjin-Minami Station|ja|天神南駅}} 4-minute walk * {{ill|Nishitetsu Bus|de|Nishitetsu Bus|ja|西鉄バス}} “Tenjin Kego Shrine – {{ill|Mitsukoshi|de|Mitsukoshi|ja|三越}} Front” 1-minute walk ==Photographs== File:Stone monument at Kego-jinja.JPG File:Stone animal monument at Kego-jinja.jpg ==External Links== {{Commonscat|Kego-jinja}} *[http://www.kegojinja.or.jp/ Kego Shrine] Juli|ja|7月19日|12=draft|12=draft}} (Natsugoshi Festival) }} {{osm box|n|384542332}} '''Kego Shrine''' (けごじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|de|Fukuoka-Präfektur|ja|福岡県|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Fukuoka City|de|Fukuoka-Stadt|ja|福岡市|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|de|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|ja|福岡市中央区 (福岡市)|lt=Chuo Ward|lt_ja=中央区|lt_de=Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|12=draft|12=draft}} located at Tenjin 2-2-20, and is a {{ill|Shrine|de|Schrein|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}}. Its former shrine Rank was {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|de|Präfektur-Schrein|ja|県社|12=draft|12=draft}}. ==Enshrined Deities== Kego Ōkami (the three deities): * {{ill|Yasomagatsuhinokami|de|Yasomagatsuhinokami|ja|禍津日神|lt=Yasomagatsuhinokami|lt_ja=八十禍津日神|lt_de=Yasomagatsuhinokami|12=draft|12=draft}} (yasomagatsuhinokami) * {{ill|Kannaobi no Kami|de|Kannaobi no Kami|ja|直毘神|lt=Kannaobi no Kami|lt_ja=神直毘神|lt_de=Kannaobi no Kami|12=draft|12=draft}} (kannaobi no kami) * {{ill|Kannaobi no Kami|de|Kannaobi no Kami|ja|直毘神|lt=Great Kannaobi no Kami|lt_ja=大直毘神|lt_de=Great Kannaobi no Kami|12=draft|12=draft}} (great kannaobi no kami) ==History== According to shrine legend, during the {{ill|Conquest of the Three Han|de|Eroberung der Drei Han|ja|三韓征伐|12=draft|12=draft}}, Empress Jingū’s fleet was protected and led to victory by Kego Ōkami, who was then enshrined at Fukasaki (around the area of the main bailey of {{ill|Fukuoka Castle|de|Fukuoka Schloss|ja|福岡城|12=draft|12=draft}}), marking the beginning of the shrine. In the 6th year of the {{ill|Keichō era|de|Keichō-Zeit|ja|慶長|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|1601|de|1601|ja|1601年|12=draft|12=draft}}), during the construction of Fukuoka Castle, the shrine was moved to Shimokego Village (in the area around present-day {{ill|Fukuoka City|de|Fukuoka-Stadt|ja|福岡市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|de|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|ja|福岡市中央区 (福岡市)|lt=Chuo Ward|lt_ja=中央区|lt_de=Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tenjin|de|Tenjin|ja|天神 (福岡市)|lt=Tenjin|lt_ja=天神|lt_de=Tenjin|12=draft|12=draft}})). In the 13th year of the {{ill|Keichō era|de|Keichō-Zeit|ja|慶長|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|1608|de|1608|ja|1608年|12=draft|12=draft}}), {{ill|Kuroda Nagamasa|de|Kuroda Nagamasa|ja|黒田長政|12=draft|12=draft}}, the lord of Fukuoka Castle, had a shrine building erected at its current location. In the 5th year of the {{ill|Taishō era|de|Taishō-Zeit|ja|大正|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|1916|de|1916|ja|1916年|12=draft|12=draft}}), it was Ranked as a Prefectural Shrine. The shrine’s name and the surrounding area “Kego” derive from the former government office “Kegosho” that was once located in the {{ill|Kōrokukan|de|Kōrokukan|ja|鴻臚館|12=draft|12=draft}}. ==Facilities== [[File:Haiden of Kego Shrine 2.jpg|thumb|240px|{{ill|Haiden|de|Haiden|ja|拝殿|12=draft|12=draft}}]] [[File:Shimmon Gate of Kego Shrine.JPG|thumb|240px|{{ill|Shinmon Gate|de|Shinmon {{Shrine |Name = Kego Shrine (警固神社) |image = [[File:Torii of Kego Shrine.JPG|250px|{{ill|Kego Shrine Approach|de|Kego Shrine Approach|ja|警固神社参道|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}]]
Torii and Worship Hall |Location = {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|de|Fukuoka-Präfektur|ja|福岡県|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Fukuoka City|de|Fukuoka-Stadt|ja|福岡市|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|de|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|ja|福岡市中央区 (福岡市)|lt=Chuo Ward|lt_ja=中央区|lt_de=Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Tenjin 2-2-20 |Latitude degrees = 33 | Latitude minutes = 35 | Latitude seconds = 16 | N (North Latitude) = N |Longitude degrees = 130 | Longitude minutes = 23 | Longitude seconds = 59.7 | E (East Longitude) = E |Map Country Code = JP |Map = Fukuoka City |Enshrined Deities = Kego Ōkami ({{ill|Kannaobi no Kami|de|Kannaobi no Kami|ja|神直毘神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ・ {{ill|Kannaobi no Kami|de|Kannaobi no Kami|ja|直毘神|lt=Great Kannaobi no Kami|lt_ja=大直毘神|lt_de=Kannaobi no Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ・ {{ill|Yasomagatsuhinokami|de|Yasomagatsuhinokami|ja|禍津日神|lt=Yasomagatsuhinokami|lt_ja=八十禍津日神|lt_de=Yasomagatsuhinokami|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) |Founded = {{ill|Emperor Chūai|de|Kaiser Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 9th year |Status = Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|de|Präfektur-Schrein|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Festival = Saturdays and Sundays after {{ill|July 19|de|19. Juli|ja|7月19日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Natsugoshi Festival) }} {{osm box|n|384542332}} '''Kego Shrine''' (けごじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|de|Fukuoka-Präfektur|ja|福岡県|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Fukuoka City|de|Fukuoka-Stadt|ja|福岡市|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|de|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|ja|福岡市中央区 (福岡市)|lt=Chuo Ward|lt_ja=中央区|lt_de=Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located at Tenjin 2-2-20, and is a {{ill|Shrine|de|Schrein|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Its former shrine Rank was {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|de|Präfektur-Schrein|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. ==Enshrined Deities== Kego Ōkami (the three deities): * {{ill|Yasomagatsuhinokami|de|Yasomagatsuhinokami|ja|禍津日神|lt=Yasomagatsuhinokami|lt_ja=八十禍津日神|lt_de=Yasomagatsuhinokami|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (yasomagatsuhinokami) * {{ill|Kannaobi no Kami|de|Kannaobi no Kami|ja|直毘神|lt=Kannaobi no Kami|lt_ja=神直毘神|lt_de=Kannaobi no Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (kannaobi no kami) * {{ill|Kannaobi no Kami|de|Kannaobi no Kami|ja|直毘神|lt=Great Kannaobi no Kami|lt_ja=大直毘神|lt_de=Great Kannaobi no Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (great kannaobi no kami) ==History== According to shrine legend, during the {{ill|Conquest of the Three Han|de|Eroberung der Drei Han|ja|三韓征伐|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Empress Jingū’s fleet was protected and led to victory by Kego Ōkami, who was then enshrined at Fukasaki (around the area of the main bailey of {{ill|Fukuoka Castle|de|Fukuoka Schloss|ja|福岡城|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), marking the beginning of the shrine. In the 6th year of the {{ill|Keichō era|de|Keichō-Zeit|ja|慶長|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|1601|de|1601|ja|1601年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), during the construction of Fukuoka Castle, the shrine was moved to Shimokego Village (in the area around present-day {{ill|Fukuoka City|de|Fukuoka-Stadt|ja|福岡市|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|de|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|ja|福岡市中央区 (福岡市)|lt=Chuo Ward|lt_ja=中央区|lt_de=Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Tenjin|de|Tenjin|ja|天神 (福岡市)|lt=Tenjin|lt_ja=天神|lt_de=Tenjin|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}})). In the 13th year of the {{ill|Keichō era|de|Keichō-Zeit|ja|慶長|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|1608|de|1608|ja|1608年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), {{ill|Kuroda Nagamasa|de|Kuroda Nagamasa|ja|黒田長政|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the lord of Fukuoka Castle, had a shrine building erected at its current location. In the 5th year of the {{ill|Taishō era|de|Taishō-Zeit|ja|大正|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|1916|de|1916|ja|1916年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), it was Ranked as a Prefectural Shrine. The shrine’s name and the surrounding area “Kego” derive from the former government office “Kegosho” that was once located in the {{ill|Kōrokukan|de|Kōrokukan|ja|鴻臚館|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. ==Facilities== [[File:Haiden of Kego Shrine 2.jpg|thumb|240px|{{ill|Haiden|de|Haiden|ja|拝殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}]] [[File:Shimmon Gate of Kego Shrine.JPG|thumb|240px|{{ill|Shinmon Gate|de|Shinmon Gate|ja|神門|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}]] * Haiden (Worship Hall) * Honden (Main Hall) * Great Torii * Shinmon Gate * Harinoki Monument * Ima Masu Inari Shrine * Shintoku Hall (rental venue) {{ill|Kego Park|de|Kego Park|ja|警固公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} borders the northern side of the shrine. ==Access== * {{ill|Nishi-Nippon Railroad|de|Nishi-Nippon Railroad|ja|西日本鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Tenjin Omuta Line|de|Tenjin Omuta Line|ja|西鉄天神大牟田線|lt=Tenjin Omuta Line|lt_ja=天神大牟田線|lt_de=Tenjin Omuta Line|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Its former shrine Rank was {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|de|Präfektur-Schrein|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}}. ==Enshrined Deities== Kego Ōkami (the three deities): * {{ill|Yasomagatsuhinokami|de|Yasomagatsuhinokami|ja|禍津日神|lt=Yasomagatsuhinokami|lt_ja=八十禍津日神|lt_de=Yasomagatsuhinokami|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasomagatsuhinokami|qq=}} (yasomagatsuhinokami) * {{ill|Kannaobi no Kami|de|Kannaobi no Kami|ja|直毘神|lt=Kannaobi no Kami|lt_ja=神直毘神|lt_de=Kannaobi no Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannaobi no Kami|qq=}} (kannaobi no kami) * {{ill|Kannaobi no Kami|de|Kannaobi no Kami|ja|直毘神|lt=Great Kannaobi no Kami|lt_ja=大直毘神|lt_de=Great Kannaobi no Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannaobi no Kami|qq=}} (great kannaobi no kami) ==History== According to shrine legend, during the {{ill|Conquest of the Three Han|de|Eroberung der Drei Han|ja|三韓征伐|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Conquest of the Three Han|qq=}}, Empress Jingū’s fleet was protected and led to victory by Kego Ōkami, who was then enshrined at Fukasaki (around the area of the main bailey of {{ill|Fukuoka Castle|de|Fukuoka Schloss|ja|福岡城|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka Castle|qq=}}), marking the beginning of the shrine. In the 6th year of the {{ill|Keichō era|de|Keichō-Zeit|ja|慶長|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keichō era|qq=}} ({{ill|1601|de|1601|ja|1601年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1601|qq=}}), during the construction of Fukuoka Castle, the shrine was moved to Shimokego Village (in the area around present-day {{ill|Fukuoka City|de|Fukuoka-Stadt|ja|福岡市|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka City|qq=}}, {{ill|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|de|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|ja|福岡市中央区 (福岡市)|lt=Chuo Ward|lt_ja=中央区|lt_de=Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|qq=}}, {{ill|Tenjin|de|Tenjin|ja|天神 (福岡市)|lt=Tenjin|lt_ja=天神|lt_de=Tenjin|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjin|qq=}})). In the 13th year of the {{ill|Keichō era|de|Keichō-Zeit|ja|慶長|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keichō era|qq=}} ({{ill|1608|de|1608|ja|1608年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1608|qq=}}), {{ill|Kuroda Nagamasa|de|Kuroda Nagamasa|ja|黒田長政|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Nagamasa|qq=}}, the lord of Fukuoka Castle, had a shrine building erected at its current location. In the 5th year of the {{ill|Taishō era|de|Taishō-Zeit|ja|大正|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishō era|qq=}} ({{ill|1916|de|1916|ja|1916年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1916|qq=}}), it was Ranked as a Prefectural Shrine. The shrine’s name and the surrounding area “Kego” derive from the former government office “Kegosho” that was once located in the {{ill|Kōrokukan|de|Kōrokukan|ja|鴻臚館|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōrokukan|qq=}}. ==Facilities== [[File:Haiden of Kego Shrine 2.jpg|thumb|240px|{{ill|Haiden|de|Haiden|ja|拝殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haiden|qq=}}]] [[File:Shimmon Gate of Kego Shrine.JPG|thumb|240px|{{ill|Shinmon Gate|de|Shinmon Gate|ja|神門|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmon Gate|qq=}}]] * Haiden (Worship Hall) * Honden (Main Hall) * Great Torii * Shinmon Gate * Harinoki Monument * Ima Masu Inari Shrine * Shintoku Hall (rental venue) {{ill|Kego Park|de|Kego Park|ja|警固公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kego Park|qq=}} borders the northern side of the shrine. ==Access== * {{ill|Nishi-Nippon Railroad|de|Nishi-Nippon Railroad|ja|西日本鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishi-Nippon Railroad|qq=}} {{ill|Tenjin Omuta Line|de|Tenjin Omuta Torii and Worship Hall |Location = [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[:en:Fukuoka|Fukuoka City]] {{ill|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|de|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|ja|福岡市中央区 (福岡市)|lt=Chuo Ward|lt_ja=中央区|lt_de=Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|qq=}} Tenjin 2-2-20 |Latitude degrees = 33 | Latitude minutes = 35 | Latitude seconds = 16 | N (North Latitude) = N |Longitude degrees = 130 | Longitude minutes = 23 | Longitude seconds = 59.7 | E (East Longitude) = E |Map Country Code = JP |Map = Fukuoka City |Enshrined Deities = Kego Ōkami ({{ill|Kannaobi no Kami|de|Kannaobi no Kami|ja|神直毘神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannaobi no Kami|qq=}} ・ {{ill|Kannaobi no Kami|de|Kannaobi no Kami|ja|直毘神|lt=Great Kannaobi no Kami|lt_ja=大直毘神|lt_de=Kannaobi no Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannaobi no Kami|qq=}} ・ {{ill|Yasomagatsuhinokami|de|Yasomagatsuhinokami|ja|禍津日神|lt=Yasomagatsuhinokami|lt_ja=八十禍津日神|lt_de=Yasomagatsuhinokami|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasomagatsuhinokami|qq=}}) |Founded = [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]] 9th year |Status = Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|de|Präfektur-Schrein|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}} |Festival = Saturdays and Sundays after [[:en:July 19|July 19]] (Natsugoshi Festival) }} {{osm box|n|384542332}} '''Kego Shrine''' (けごじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[:en:Fukuoka|Fukuoka City]] {{ill|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|de|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|ja|福岡市中央区 (福岡市)|lt=Chuo Ward|lt_ja=中央区|lt_de=Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|qq=}} located at Tenjin 2-2-20, and is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shrine]]. Its former shrine Rank was {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|de|Präfektur-Schrein|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}}. ==Enshrined Deities== Kego Ōkami (the three deities): * {{ill|Yasomagatsuhinokami|de|Yasomagatsuhinokami|ja|禍津日神|lt=Yasomagatsuhinokami|lt_ja=八十禍津日神|lt_de=Yasomagatsuhinokami|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasomagatsuhinokami|qq=}} (yasomagatsuhinokami) * {{ill|Kannaobi no Kami|de|Kannaobi no Kami|ja|直毘神|lt=Kannaobi no Kami|lt_ja=神直毘神|lt_de=Kannaobi no Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannaobi no Kami|qq=}} (kannaobi no kami) * {{ill|Kannaobi no Kami|de|Kannaobi no Kami|ja|直毘神|lt=Great Kannaobi no Kami|lt_ja=大直毘神|lt_de=Great Kannaobi no Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannaobi no Kami|qq=}} (great kannaobi no kami) ==History== According to shrine legend, during the {{ill|Conquest of the Three Han|de|Eroberung der Drei Han|ja|三韓征伐|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Conquest of the Three Han|qq=}}, Empress Jingū’s fleet was protected and led to victory by Kego Ōkami, who was then enshrined at Fukasaki (around the area of the main bailey of [[:en:Fukuoka Castle|Fukuoka Castle]]), marking the beginning of the shrine. In the 6th year of the [[:en:Keichō|Keichō era]] ([[:en:1601|1601]]), during the construction of Fukuoka Castle, the shrine was moved to Shimokego Village (in the area around present-day [[:en:Fukuoka|Fukuoka City]], {{ill|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|de|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|ja|福岡市中央区 (福岡市)|lt=Chuo Ward|lt_ja=中央区|lt_de=Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|qq=}}, {{ill|Tenjin|de|Tenjin|ja|天神 (福岡市)|lt=Tenjin|lt_ja=天神|lt_de=Tenjin|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjin|qq=}})). In the 13th year of the [[:en:Keichō|Keichō era]] ([[:en:1608|1608]]), [[:en:Kuroda Nagamasa|Kuroda Nagamasa]], the lord of Fukuoka Castle, had a shrine building erected at its current location. In the 5th year of the [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō era]] ([[:en:1916|1916]]), it was Ranked as a Prefectural Shrine. The shrine’s name and the surrounding area “Kego” derive from the former government office “Kegosho” that was once located in the [[:en:Kōrokan|Kōrokukan]]. ==Facilities== [[File:Haiden of Kego Shrine 2.jpg|thumb|240px|[[:en:Haiden (Shinto)|Haiden]]]] [[File:Shimmon Gate of Kego Shrine.JPG|thumb|240px|{{ill|Shinmon Gate|de|Shinmon Gate|ja|神門|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmon Gate|qq=}}]] * Haiden (Worship Hall) * Honden (Main Hall) * Great Torii * Shinmon Gate * Harinoki Monument * Ima Masu Inari Shrine * Shintoku Hall (rental venue) {{ill|Kego Park|de|Kego Park|ja|警固公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kego Park|qq=}} borders the northern side of the shrine. ==Access== * [[:en:Nishi-Nippon Railroad|Nishi-Nippon Railroad]] [[:en:Nishitetsu Tenjin Ōmuta Line|Tenjin Omuta Line]] [[:en:Nishitetsu Fukuoka (Tenjin) Station|Nishitetsu Fukuoka (Tenjin) Station]] (South Exit) 1-minute walk * {{ill|Fukuoka City Subway|de|Fukuoka City Subway|ja|福岡市交通局|lt=Fukuoka City Subway|lt_ja=福岡市地下鉄|lt_de=Fukuoka City Subway|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka City Subway|qq=}} [[:en:Kūkō Line (Fukuoka City Subway)|Airport Line]] [[:en:Tenjin Station|Tenjin Station]] 7-minute walk * Fukuoka City Subway [[:en:Nanakuma Line|Nanakuma Line]] [[:en:Tenjin-Minami Station|Tenjin-Minami Station]] 4-minute walk * {{ill|Nishitetsu Bus|de|Nishitetsu Bus|ja|西鉄バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitetsu Bus|qq=}} “Tenjin Kego Shrine – [[:en:Mitsukoshi|Mitsukoshi]] Front” 1-minute walk ==Photographs== File:Stone monument at Kego-jinja.JPG File:Stone animal monument at Kego-jinja.jpg ==External Links== {{Commonscat|Kego-jinja}} *[http://www.kegojinja.or.jp/ Kego Shrine] (official website) {{Shinto Side}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{translated page|ja|警固神社|version=103161857|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrines in Fukuoka City]] [[Category:History of Chuo Ward, Fukuoka City]] [[Category:Buildings in Chuo Ward, Fukuoka City]] [[Category:Fukuoka Tenjin]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:警固神社]] [[de:Kego Shrine]] [[ja:%E8%AD%A6%E5%9B%BA%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:Kego Shrine]]

revision.text:
{{Shrine |Name = Kego Shrine (警固神社) |image = [[File:Torii of Kego Shrine.JPG|250px|{{ill|Kego Shrine Approach|de|Kego Shrine Approach|ja|警固神社参道|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kego Shrine Approach|qq=}}]]
Torii and Worship Hall |Location = [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[:en:Fukuoka|Fukuoka City]] {{ill|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|de|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|ja|福岡市中央区 (福岡市)|lt=Chuo Ward|lt_ja=中央区|lt_de=Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|qq=}} Tenjin 2-2-20 |Latitude degrees = 33 | Latitude minutes = 35 | Latitude seconds = 16 | N (North Latitude) = N |Longitude degrees = 130 | Longitude minutes = 23 | Longitude seconds = 59.7 | E (East Longitude) = E |Map Country Code = JP |Map = Fukuoka City |Enshrined Deities = Kego Ōkami ({{ill|Kannaobi no Kami|de|Kannaobi no Kami|ja|神直毘神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannaobi no Kami|qq=}} ・ {{ill|Kannaobi no Kami|de|Kannaobi no Kami|ja|直毘神|lt=Great Kannaobi no Kami|lt_ja=大直毘神|lt_de=Kannaobi no Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannaobi no Kami|qq=}} ・ {{ill|Yasomagatsuhinokami|de|Yasomagatsuhinokami|ja|禍津日神|lt=Yasomagatsuhinokami|lt_ja=八十禍津日神|lt_de=Yasomagatsuhinokami|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasomagatsuhinokami|qq=}}) |Founded = [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]] 9th year |Status = Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|de|Präfektur-Schrein|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}} |Festival = Saturdays and Sundays after [[:en:July 19|July 19]] (Natsugoshi Festival) }} {{osm box|n|384542332}} '''Kego Shrine''' (けごじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[:en:Fukuoka|Fukuoka City]] {{ill|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|de|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|ja|福岡市中央区 (福岡市)|lt=Chuo Ward|lt_ja=中央区|lt_de=Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|qq=}} located at Tenjin 2-2-20, and is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shrine]]. Its former shrine Rank was {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|de|Präfektur-Schrein|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}}. ==Enshrined Deities== Kego Ōkami (the three deities): * {{ill|Yasomagatsuhinokami|de|Yasomagatsuhinokami|ja|禍津日神|lt=Yasomagatsuhinokami|lt_ja=八十禍津日神|lt_de=Yasomagatsuhinokami|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasomagatsuhinokami|qq=}} (yasomagatsuhinokami) * {{ill|Kannaobi no Kami|de|Kannaobi no Kami|ja|直毘神|lt=Kannaobi no Kami|lt_ja=神直毘神|lt_de=Kannaobi no Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannaobi no Kami|qq=}} (kannaobi no kami) * {{ill|Kannaobi no Kami|de|Kannaobi no Kami|ja|直毘神|lt=Great Kannaobi no Kami|lt_ja=大直毘神|lt_de=Great Kannaobi no Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannaobi no Kami|qq=}} (great kannaobi no kami) ==History== According to shrine legend, during the {{ill|Conquest of the Three Han|de|Eroberung der Drei Han|ja|三韓征伐|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Conquest of the Three Han|qq=}}, Empress Jingū’s fleet was protected and led to victory by Kego Ōkami, who was then enshrined at Fukasaki (around the area of the main bailey of [[:en:Fukuoka Castle|Fukuoka Castle]]), marking the beginning of the shrine. In the 6th year of the [[:en:Keichō|Keichō era]] ([[:en:1601|1601]]), during the construction of Fukuoka Castle, the shrine was moved to Shimokego Village (in the area around present-day [[:en:Fukuoka|Fukuoka City]], {{ill|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|de|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|ja|福岡市中央区 (福岡市)|lt=Chuo Ward|lt_ja=中央区|lt_de=Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|qq=}}, {{ill|Tenjin|de|Tenjin|ja|天神 (福岡市)|lt=Tenjin|lt_ja=天神|lt_de=Tenjin|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjin|qq=}})). In the 13th year of the [[:en:Keichō|Keichō era]] ([[:en:1608|1608]]), [[:en:Kuroda Nagamasa|Kuroda Nagamasa]], the lord of Fukuoka Castle, had a shrine building erected at its current location. In the 5th year of the [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō era]] ([[:en:1916|1916]]), it was Ranked as a Prefectural Shrine. The shrine’s name and the surrounding area “Kego” derive from the former government office “Kegosho” that was once located in the [[:en:Kōrokan|Kōrokukan]]. ==Facilities== [[File:Haiden of Kego Shrine 2.jpg|thumb|240px|[[:en:Haiden (Shinto)|Haiden]]]] [[File:Shimmon Gate of Kego Shrine.JPG|thumb|240px|{{ill|Shinmon Gate|de|Shinmon Gate|ja|神門|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmon Gate|qq=}}]] * Haiden (Worship Hall) * Honden (Main Hall) * Great Torii * Shinmon Gate * Harinoki Monument * Ima Masu Inari Shrine * Shintoku Hall (rental venue) {{ill|Kego Park|de|Kego Park|ja|警固公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kego Park|qq=}} borders the northern side of the shrine. ==Access== * Station|Tenjin-Minami Station]] 4-minute walk * {{ill|Nishitetsu Bus|de|Nishitetsu Bus|ja|西鉄バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitetsu Bus|qq=}} “Tenjin Kego Shrine – revision.comment: Bot: update category → Shrines in Fukuoka
revision.text:
{{Shrine |Name = Kego Shrine (警固神社) |image = [[File:Torii of Kego Shrine.JPG|250px|{{ill|Kego Shrine Approach|de|Kego Shrine Approach|ja|警固神社参道|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kego Shrine Approach|qq=}}]]
Torii and Worship Hall |Location = [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[:en:Fukuoka|Fukuoka City]] {{ill|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|de|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|ja|福岡市中央区 (福岡市)|lt=Chuo Ward|lt_ja=中央区|lt_de=Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|qq=}} Tenjin 2-2-20 |Latitude degrees = 33 | Latitude minutes = 35 | Latitude seconds = 16 | N (North Latitude) = N |Longitude degrees = 130 | Longitude minutes = 23 | Longitude seconds = 59.7 | E (East Longitude) = E |Map Country Code = JP |Map = Fukuoka City |Enshrined Deities = Kego Ōkami ({{ill|Kannaobi no Kami|de|Kannaobi no Kami|ja|神直毘神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannaobi no Kami|qq=}} ・ {{ill|Kannaobi no Kami|de|Kannaobi no Kami|ja|直毘神|lt=Great Kannaobi no Kami|lt_ja=大直毘神|lt_de=Kannaobi no Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannaobi no Kami|qq=}} ・ {{ill|Yasomagatsuhinokami|de|Yasomagatsuhinokami|ja|禍津日神|lt=Yasomagatsuhinokami|lt_ja=八十禍津日神|lt_de=Yasomagatsuhinokami|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasomagatsuhinokami|qq=}}) |Founded = [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]] 9th year |Status = Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|de|Präfektur-Schrein|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}} |Festival = Saturdays and Sundays after [[:en:July 19|July 19]] (Natsugoshi Festival) }} {{osm box|n|384542332}} '''Kego Shrine''' (けごじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[:en:Fukuoka|Fukuoka City]] {{ill|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|de|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|ja|福岡市中央区 (福岡市)|lt=Chuo Ward|lt_ja=中央区|lt_de=Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|qq=}} located at Tenjin 2-2-20, and is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shrine]]. Its former shrine Rank was {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|de|Präfektur-Schrein|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}}. ==Enshrined Deities== Kego Ōkami (the three deities): * {{ill|Yasomagatsuhinokami|de|Yasomagatsuhinokami|ja|禍津日神|lt=Yasomagatsuhinokami|lt_ja=八十禍津日神|lt_de=Yasomagatsuhinokami|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasomagatsuhinokami|qq=}} (yasomagatsuhinokami) * {{ill|Kannaobi no Kami|de|Kannaobi no Kami|ja|直毘神|lt=Kannaobi no Kami|lt_ja=神直毘神|lt_de=Kannaobi no Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannaobi no Kami|qq=}} (kannaobi no kami) * {{ill|Kannaobi no Kami|de|Kannaobi no Kami|ja|直毘神|lt=Great Kannaobi no Kami|lt_ja=大直毘神|lt_de=Great Kannaobi no Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannaobi no Kami|qq=}} (great kannaobi no kami) ==History== According to shrine legend, during the {{ill|Conquest of the Three Han|de|Eroberung der Drei Han|ja|三韓征伐|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Conquest of the Three Han|qq=}}, Empress Jingū’s fleet was protected and led to victory by Kego Ōkami, who was then enshrined at Fukasaki (around the area of the main bailey of [[:en:Fukuoka Castle|Fukuoka Castle]]), marking the beginning of the shrine. In the 6th year of the [[:en:Keichō|Keichō era]] ([[:en:1601|1601]]), during the construction of Fukuoka Castle, the shrine was moved to Shimokego Village (in the area around present-day [[:en:Fukuoka|Fukuoka City]], {{ill|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|de|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|ja|福岡市中央区 (福岡市)|lt=Chuo Ward|lt_ja=中央区|lt_de=Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|qq=}}, {{ill|Tenjin|de|Tenjin|ja|天神 (福岡市)|lt=Tenjin|lt_ja=天神|lt_de=Tenjin|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjin|qq=}})). In the 13th year of the [[:en:Keichō|Keichō era]] ([[:en:1608|1608]]), [[:en:Kuroda Nagamasa|Kuroda Nagamasa]], the lord of Fukuoka Castle, had a shrine building erected at its current location. In the 5th year of the [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō era]] ([[:en:1916|1916]]), it was Ranked as a Prefectural Shrine. The shrine’s name and the surrounding area “Kego” derive from the former government office “Kegosho” that was once located in the [[:en:Kōrokan|Kōrokukan]]. ==Facilities== [[File:Haiden of Kego Shrine 2.jpg|thumb|240px|[[:en:Haiden (Shinto)|Haiden]]]] [[File:Shimmon Gate of Kego Shrine.JPG|thumb|240px|{{ill|Shinmon Gate|de|Shinmon Gate|ja|神門|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmon Gate|qq=}}]] * Haiden (Worship Hall) * Honden (Main Hall) * Great Torii * Shinmon Gate * Harinoki Monument * Ima Masu Inari Shrine * Shintoku Hall (rental venue) {{ill|Kego Park|de|Kego Park|ja|警固公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kego Park|qq=}} borders the northern side of the shrine. ==Access== * Station|Tenjin-Minami Station]] 4-minute walk * {{ill|Nishitetsu Bus|de|Nishitetsu Bus|ja|西鉄バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitetsu Bus|qq=}} “Tenjin Kego Shrine – In the 6th year of the [[:en:Keichō|Keichō era]] ([[:en:1601|1601]]), during the construction of Fukuoka Castle, the shrine was moved to Shimokego Village (in the area around present-day [[:en:Fukuoka|Fukuoka City]], {{ill|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|de|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|ja|福岡市中央区 (福岡市)|lt=Chuo Ward|lt_ja=中央区|lt_de=Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|qq=}}, {{ill|Tenjin|de|Tenjin|ja|天神 (福岡市)|lt=Tenjin|lt_ja=天神|lt_de=Tenjin|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjin|qq=}})). In the 13th year of the [[:en:Keichō|Keichō era]] ([[:en:1608|1608]]), [[:en:Kuroda Nagamasa|Kuroda Nagamasa]], the lord of Fukuoka Castle, had a shrine building erected at its current location. In the 5th year of the [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō era]] ([[:en:1916|1916]]), it was Ranked as a Prefectural Shrine. The shrine’s name and the surrounding area “Kego” derive from the former government office “Kegosho” that was once located in the [[:en:Kōrokan|Kōrokukan]]. ==Facilities== [[File:Haiden of Kego Shrine 2.jpg|thumb|240px|[[:en:Haiden (Shinto)|Haiden]]]] [[File:Shimmon Gate of Kego Shrine.JPG|thumb|240px|{{ill|Shinmon Gate|de|Shinmon Gate|ja|神門|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmon Gate|qq=}}]] * Haiden (Worship Hall) * Honden (Main Hall) * Great Torii * Shinmon Gate * Harinoki Monument * Ima Masu Inari Shrine * Shintoku Hall (rental venue) {{ill|Kego Park|de|Kego Park|ja|警固公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kego Park|qq=}} borders the northern side of the shrine. ==Access== * 1-minute walk * {{ill|Fukuoka City Subway|de|Fukuoka City Subway|ja|福岡市交通局|lt=Fukuoka City Subway|lt_ja=福岡市地下鉄|lt_de=Fukuoka City Subway|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka City Subway|qq=}} [[:en:Kūkō Line (Fukuoka City Subway)|Airport Line]] [[:en:Tenjin Station|Tenjin Station]] 7-minute walk * Fukuoka City Subway [[:en:Nanakuma Line|Nanakuma Line]] [[:en:Tenjin-Minami Station|Tenjin-Minami Station]] 4-minute walk * {{ill|Nishitetsu Bus|de|Nishitetsu Bus|ja|西鉄バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitetsu Bus|qq=}} “Tenjin Kego Shrine – [[:en:Mitsukoshi|Mitsukoshi]] Front” 1-minute walk ==Photographs== File:Stone monument at Kego-jinja.JPG File:Stone animal monument at Kego-jinja.jpg ==External Links== {{Commonscat|Kego-jinja}}
