{{Shrine |Name = Kego Shrine (警固神社) |image = [[File:Torii of Kego Shrine.JPG|250px|{{ill|Kego Shrine Approach|de|Kego Shrine Approach|ja|警固神社参道|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kego Shrine Approach|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}]]
Torii and Worship Hall |Location = [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[:en:Fukuoka|Fukuoka City]] {{ill|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|de|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|ja|福岡市中央区 (福岡市)|lt=Chuo Ward|lt_ja=中央区|lt_de=Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Tenjin 2-2-20 |Latitude degrees = 33 | Latitude minutes = 35 | Latitude seconds = 16 | N (North Latitude) = N |Longitude degrees = 130 | Longitude minutes = 23 | Longitude seconds = 59.7 | E (East Longitude) = E |Map Country Code = JP |Map = Fukuoka City |Enshrined Deities = Kego Ōkami ({{ill|Kannaobi no Kami|de|Kannaobi no Kami|ja|神直毘神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannaobi no Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神直日神}} ・ {{ill|Kannaobi no Kami|de|Kannaobi no Kami|ja|直毘神|lt=Great Kannaobi no Kami|lt_ja=大直毘神|lt_de=Kannaobi no Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannaobi no Kami|qq=}} ・ {{ill|Yasomagatsuhinokami|de|Yasomagatsuhinokami|ja|禍津日神|lt=Yasomagatsuhinokami|lt_ja=八十禍津日神|lt_de=Yasomagatsuhinokami|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasomagatsuhinokami|qq=}}) |Founded = [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]] 9th year |Status = Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|de|Präfektur-Schrein|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} |Festival = Saturdays and Sundays after [[:en:July 19|July 19]] (Natsugoshi Festival) }} {{osm box|n|384542332}} '''Kego Shrine''' (けごじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[:en:Fukuoka|Fukuoka City]] {{ill|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|de|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|ja|福岡市中央区 (福岡市)|lt=Chuo Ward|lt_ja=中央区|lt_de=Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} located at Tenjin 2-2-20, and is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shrine]]. Its former shrine Rank was {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|de|Präfektur-Schrein|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. ==Enshrined Deities== Kego Ōkami (the three deities): * {{ill|Yasomagatsuhinokami|de|Yasomagatsuhinokami|ja|禍津日神|lt=Yasomagatsuhinokami|lt_ja=八十禍津日神|lt_de=Yasomagatsuhinokami|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasomagatsuhinokami|qq=}} (yasomagatsuhinokami) * {{ill|Kannaobi no Kami|de|Kannaobi no Kami|ja|直毘神|lt=Kannaobi no Kami|lt_ja=神直毘神|lt_de=Kannaobi no Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannaobi no Kami|qq=}} (kannaobi no kami) * {{ill|Kannaobi no Kami|de|Kannaobi no Kami|ja|直毘神|lt=Great Kannaobi no Kami|lt_ja=大直毘神|lt_de=Great Kannaobi no Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannaobi no Kami|qq=}} (great kannaobi no kami) ==History== According to shrine legend, during the {{ill|Conquest of the Three Han|de|Eroberung der Drei Han|ja|三韓征伐|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Conquest of the Three Han|qq=}}, Empress Jingū’s fleet was protected and led to victory by Kego Ōkami, who was then enshrined at Fukasaki (around the area of the main bailey of [[:en:Fukuoka Castle|Fukuoka Castle]]), marking the beginning of the shrine. In the 6th year of the [[:en:Keichō|Keichō era]] ([[:en:1601|1601]]), during the construction of Fukuoka Castle, the shrine was moved to Shimokego Village (in the area around present-day [[:en:Fukuoka|Fukuoka City]], {{ill|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|de|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|ja|福岡市中央区 (福岡市)|lt=Chuo Ward|lt_ja=中央区|lt_de=Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Tenjin (kami)|Tenjin]])). In the 13th year of the [[:en:Keichō|Keichō era]] ([[:en:1608|1608]]), [[:en:Kuroda Nagamasa|Kuroda Nagamasa]], the lord of Fukuoka Castle, had a shrine building erected at its current location. In the 5th year of the [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō era]] ([[:en:1916|1916]]), it was Ranked as a Prefectural Shrine. The shrine’s name and the surrounding area “Kego” derive from the former government office “Kegosho” that was once located in the [[:en:Kōrokan|Kōrokukan]]. ==Facilities== [[File:Haiden of Kego Shrine 2.jpg|thumb|240px|[[:en:Haiden (Shinto)|Haiden]]]] [[File:Shimmon Gate of Kego Shrine.JPG|thumb|240px|{{ill|Shinmon Gate|de|Shinmon Gate|ja|神門|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmon Gate|qq=}}]] * Haiden (Worship Hall) * Honden (Main Hall) * Great Torii * Shinmon Gate * Harinoki Monument * Ima Masu Inari Shrine * Shintoku Hall (rental venue) {{ill|Kego Park|de|Kego Park|ja|警固公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kego Park|qq=}} borders the northern side of the shrine. ==Access== * Bus|ja|西鉄バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitetsu Bus|qq=}} “Tenjin Kego Shrine – [[:en:Mitsukoshi|Mitsukoshi]] Front” 1-minute walk ==Photographs== File:Stone monument at {{Shrine |Name = Kego Shrine (警固神社) |image = [[File:Torii of Kego Shrine.JPG|250px|{{ill|Kego Shrine Approach|de|Kego Shrine Approach|ja|警固神社参道|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kego Shrine Approach|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}]]
Torii and Worship Hall |Location = [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[:en:Fukuoka|Fukuoka City]] {{ill|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|de|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|ja|福岡市中央区 (福岡市)|lt=Chuo Ward|lt_ja=中央区|lt_de=Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Tenjin 2-2-20 |Latitude degrees = 33 | Latitude minutes = 35 | Latitude seconds = 16 | N (North Latitude) = N |Longitude degrees = 130 | Longitude minutes = 23 | Longitude seconds = 59.7 | E (East Longitude) = E |Map Country Code = JP |Map = Fukuoka City |Enshrined Deities = Kego Ōkami ({{ill|Kannaobi no Kami|de|Kannaobi no Kami|ja|神直毘神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannaobi no Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神直日神}} ・ {{ill|Kannaobi no Kami|de|Kannaobi no Kami|ja|直毘神|lt=Great Kannaobi no Kami|lt_ja=大直毘神|lt_de=Kannaobi no Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannaobi no Kami|qq=}} ・ {{ill|Yasomagatsuhinokami|de|Yasomagatsuhinokami|ja|禍津日神|lt=Yasomagatsuhinokami|lt_ja=八十禍津日神|lt_de=Yasomagatsuhinokami|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasomagatsuhinokami|qq=}}) |Founded = [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]] 9th year |Status = Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|de|Präfektur-Schrein|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} |Festival = Saturdays and Sundays after [[:en:July 19|July 19]] (Natsugoshi Festival) }} {{osm box|n|384542332}} '''Kego Shrine''' (けごじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[:en:Fukuoka|Fukuoka City]] {{ill|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|de|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|ja|福岡市中央区 (福岡市)|lt=Chuo Ward|lt_ja=中央区|lt_de=Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} located at Tenjin 2-2-20, and is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shrine]]. Its former shrine Rank was {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|de|Präfektur-Schrein|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. ==Enshrined Deities== Kego Ōkami (the three deities): * {{ill|Yasomagatsuhinokami|de|Yasomagatsuhinokami|ja|禍津日神|lt=Yasomagatsuhinokami|lt_ja=八十禍津日神|lt_de=Yasomagatsuhinokami|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasomagatsuhinokami|qq=}} (yasomagatsuhinokami) * {{ill|Kannaobi no Kami|de|Kannaobi no Kami|ja|直毘神|lt=Kannaobi no Kami|lt_ja=神直毘神|lt_de=Kannaobi no Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannaobi no Kami|qq=}} (kannaobi no kami) * {{ill|Kannaobi no Kami|de|Kannaobi no Kami|ja|直毘神|lt=Great Kannaobi no Kami|lt_ja=大直毘神|lt_de=Great Kannaobi no Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannaobi no Kami|qq=}} (great kannaobi no kami) ==History== According to shrine legend, during the {{ill|Conquest of the Three Han|de|Eroberung der Drei Han|ja|三韓征伐|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Conquest of the Three Han|qq=}}, Empress Jingū’s fleet was protected and led to victory by Kego Ōkami, who was then enshrined at Fukasaki (around the area of the main bailey of [[:en:Fukuoka Castle|Fukuoka Castle]]), marking the beginning of the shrine. In the 6th year of the [[:en:Keichō|Keichō era]] ([[:en:1601|1601]]), during the construction of Fukuoka Castle, the shrine was moved to Shimokego Village (in the area around present-day [[:en:Fukuoka|Fukuoka City]], {{ill|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|de|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|ja|福岡市中央区 (福岡市)|lt=Chuo Ward|lt_ja=中央区|lt_de=Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Tenjin (kami)|Tenjin]])). In the 13th year of the [[:en:Keichō|Keichō era]] ([[:en:1608|1608]]), [[:en:Kuroda Nagamasa|Kuroda Nagamasa]], the lord of Fukuoka Castle, had a shrine building erected at its current location. In the 5th year of the [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō era]] ([[:en:1916|1916]]), it was Ranked as a Prefectural Shrine. The shrine’s name and the surrounding area “Kego” derive from the former government office “Kegosho” that was once located in the [[:en:Kōrokan|Kōrokukan]]. ==Facilities== [[File:Haiden of Kego Shrine 2.jpg|thumb|240px|[[:en:Haiden (Shinto)|Haiden]]]] [[File:Shimmon Gate of Kego Shrine.JPG|thumb|240px|{{ill|Shinmon Gate|de|Shinmon Gate|ja|神門|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmon Gate|qq=}}]] * Haiden (Worship Hall) * Honden (Main Hall) * Great Torii * Shinmon Gate * Harinoki Monument * Ima Masu Inari Shrine * Shintoku Hall (rental venue) {{ill|Kego Park|de|Kego Park|ja|警固公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kego Park|qq=}} borders the northern side of the shrine. ==Access== * Its former shrine Rank was {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|de|Präfektur-Schrein|ja|県社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. ==Enshrined Deities== Kego Ōkami (the three deities): * {{ill|Yasomagatsuhinokami|de|Yasomagatsuhinokami|ja|禍津日神|lt=Yasomagatsuhinokami|lt_ja=八十禍津日神|lt_de=Yasomagatsuhinokami|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasomagatsuhinokami|qq=}} (yasomagatsuhinokami) * {{ill|Kannaobi no Kami|de|Kannaobi no Kami|ja|直毘神|lt=Kannaobi no Kami|lt_ja=神直毘神|lt_de=Kannaobi no Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannaobi no Kami|qq=}} (kannaobi no kami) * {{ill|Kannaobi no Kami|de|Kannaobi no Kami|ja|直毘神|lt=Great Kannaobi no Kami|lt_ja=大直毘神|lt_de=Great Kannaobi no Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannaobi no Kami|qq=}} (great kannaobi no kami) ==History== According to shrine legend, during the {{ill|Conquest of the Three Han|de|Eroberung der Drei Han|ja|三韓征伐|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Conquest of the Three Han|qq=}}, Empress Jingū’s fleet was protected and led to victory by Kego Ōkami, who was then enshrined at Fukasaki (around the area of the main bailey of [[:en:Fukuoka Castle|Fukuoka Castle]]), marking the beginning of the shrine. In the 6th year of the [[:en:Keichō|Keichō era]] ([[:en:1601|1601]]), during the construction of Fukuoka Castle, the shrine was moved to Shimokego Village (in the area around present-day [[:en:Fukuoka|Fukuoka City]], {{ill|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|de|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|ja|福岡市中央区 (福岡市)|lt=Chuo Ward|lt_ja=中央区|lt_de=Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Tenjin (kami)|Tenjin]])). In the 13th year of the [[:en:Keichō|Keichō era]] ([[:en:1608|1608]]), [[:en:Kuroda Nagamasa|Kuroda Nagamasa]], the lord of Fukuoka Castle, had a shrine building erected at its current location. In the 5th year of the [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō era]] ([[:en:1916|1916]]), it was Ranked as a Prefectural Shrine. The shrine’s name and the surrounding area “Kego” derive from the former government office “Kegosho” that was once located in the [[:en:Kōrokan|Kōrokukan]]. ==Facilities== [[File:Haiden of Kego Shrine 2.jpg|thumb|240px|[[:en:Haiden (Shinto)|Haiden]]]] [[File:Shimmon Gate of Kego Shrine.JPG|thumb|240px|{{ill|Shinmon Gate|de|Shinmon Gate|ja|神門|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmon Gate|qq=}}]] * Haiden (Worship Hall) * Honden (Main Hall) * Great Torii * Shinmon Gate * Harinoki Monument * Ima Masu Inari Shrine * Shintoku Hall (rental venue) {{ill|Kego Park|de|Kego Park|ja|警固公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kego Park|qq=}} borders the northern side of the shrine. ==Access== * [[:en:Nishi-Nippon Railroad|Nishi-Nippon Railroad]] [[:en:Nishitetsu Tenjin Ōmuta Line|Tenjin Omuta Line]] [[:en:Nishitetsu Fukuoka (Tenjin) Station|Nishitetsu Fukuoka (Tenjin) Station]] (South Exit) 1-minute walk * {{ill|Fukuoka City Subway|de|Fukuoka City Subway|ja|福岡市交通局|lt=Fukuoka City Subway|lt_ja=福岡市地下鉄|lt_de=Fukuoka City Subway|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka City Subway|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} [[:en:Kūkō Line (Fukuoka City Subway)|Airport Line]] [[:en:Tenjin Station|Tenjin Station]] 7-minute walk * Fukuoka City Subway [[:en:Nanakuma Line|Nanakuma Line]] [[:en:Tenjin-Minami Station|Tenjin-Minami Station]] 4-minute walk * {{ill|Nishitetsu Bus|de|Nishitetsu Bus|ja|西鉄バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitetsu Bus|qq=}} “Tenjin Kego Shrine – [[:en:Mitsukoshi|Mitsukoshi]] Front” 1-minute walk ==Photographs== File:Stone monument at Kego-jinja.JPG File:Stone animal monument at Kego-jinja.jpg ==External Links== {{Commonscat|Kego-jinja}} *[http://www.kegojinja.or.jp/ Kego Shrine] (official website) {{Shinto Side}} {{shinto-stub}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11632314]] Wikidata name: Kego Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:08, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Shrine |Name = Kego Shrine (警固神社) |image = [[File:Torii of Kego Shrine.JPG|250px|{{ill|Kego Shrine Approach|de|Kego Shrine Approach|ja|警固神社参道|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kego Shrine Approach|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}]]
Torii and Worship Hall |Location = [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[:en:Fukuoka|Fukuoka City]] {{ill|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|de|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|ja|福岡市中央区 (福岡市)|lt=Chuo Ward|lt_ja=中央区|lt_de=Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Tenjin 2-2-20 |Latitude degrees = 33 | Latitude minutes = 35 | Latitude seconds = 16 | N (North Latitude) = N |Longitude degrees = 130 | Longitude minutes = 23 | Longitude seconds = 59.7 | E (East Longitude) = E |Map Country Code = JP |Map = Fukuoka City |Enshrined Deities = Kego Ōkami ({{ill|Kannaobi no Kami|de|Kannaobi no Kami|ja|神直毘神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannaobi no Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神直日神}} ・ {{ill|Kannaobi no Kami|de|Kannaobi no Kami|ja|直毘神|lt=Great Kannaobi no Kami|lt_ja=大直毘神|lt_de=Kannaobi no Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannaobi no Kami|qq=}} ・ {{ill|Yasomagatsuhinokami|de|Yasomagatsuhinokami|ja|禍津日神|lt=Yasomagatsuhinokami|lt_ja=八十禍津日神|lt_de=Yasomagatsuhinokami|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasomagatsuhinokami|qq=}}) |Founded = [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]] 9th year |Status = Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|de|Präfektur-Schrein|ja|県社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} |Festival = Saturdays and Sundays after [[:en:July 19|July 19]] (Natsugoshi Festival) }} {{osm box|n|384542332}} '''Kego Shrine''' (けごじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[:en:Fukuoka|Fukuoka City]] {{ill|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|de|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|ja|福岡市中央区 (福岡市)|lt=Chuo Ward|lt_ja=中央区|lt_de=Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} located at Tenjin 2-2-20, and is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shrine]]. Its former shrine Rank was {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|de|Präfektur-Schrein|ja|県社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. ==Enshrined Deities== Kego Ōkami (the three deities): * {{ill|Yasomagatsuhinokami|de|Yasomagatsuhinokami|ja|禍津日神|lt=Yasomagatsuhinokami|lt_ja=八十禍津日神|lt_de=Yasomagatsuhinokami|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasomagatsuhinokami|qq=}} (yasomagatsuhinokami) * {{ill|Kannaobi no Kami|de|Kannaobi no Kami|ja|直毘神|lt=Kannaobi no Kami|lt_ja=神直毘神|lt_de=Kannaobi no Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannaobi no Kami|qq=}} (kannaobi no kami) * {{ill|Kannaobi no Kami|de|Kannaobi no Kami|ja|直毘神|lt=Great Kannaobi no Kami|lt_ja=大直毘神|lt_de=Great Kannaobi no Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannaobi no Kami|qq=}} (great kannaobi no kami) ==History== According to shrine legend, during the {{ill|Conquest of the Three Han|de|Eroberung der Drei Han|ja|三韓征伐|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Conquest of the Three Han|qq=}}, Empress Jingū’s fleet was protected and led to victory by Kego Ōkami, who was then enshrined at Fukasaki (around the area of the main bailey of [[:en:Fukuoka Castle|Fukuoka Castle]]), marking the beginning of the shrine. In the 6th year of the [[:en:Keichō|Keichō era]] ([[:en:1601|1601]]), during the construction of Fukuoka Castle, the shrine was moved to Shimokego Village (in the area around present-day [[:en:Fukuoka|Fukuoka City]], {{ill|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|de|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|ja|福岡市中央区 (福岡市)|lt=Chuo Ward|lt_ja=中央区|lt_de=Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Tenjin (kami)|Tenjin]])). In the 13th year of the [[:en:Keichō|Keichō era]] ([[:en:1608|1608]]), [[:en:Kuroda Nagamasa|Kuroda Nagamasa]], the lord of Fukuoka Castle, had a shrine building erected at its current location. In the 5th year of the [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō era]] ([[:en:1916|1916]]), it was Ranked as a Prefectural Shrine. The shrine’s name and the surrounding area “Kego” derive from the former government office “Kegosho” that was once located in the [[:en:Kōrokan|Kōrokukan]]. ==Facilities== [[File:Haiden of Kego Shrine 2.jpg|thumb|240px|[[:en:Haiden (Shinto)|Haiden]]]] [[File:Shimmon Gate of Kego Shrine.JPG|thumb|240px|{{ill|Shinmon Gate|de|Shinmon Gate|ja|神門|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmon Gate|qq=}}]] * Haiden (Worship Hall) * Honden (Main Hall) * Great Torii * Shinmon Gate * Harinoki Monument * Ima Masu Inari Shrine * Shintoku Hall (rental venue) {{ill|Kego Park|de|Kego Park|ja|警固公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kego Park|qq=}} borders the northern side of the shrine. ==Access== * [[:en:Nishi-Nippon Railroad|Nishi-Nippon Railroad]] [[:en:Nishitetsu Tenjin Ōmuta Line|Tenjin Omuta Line]] [[:en:Nishitetsu Fukuoka (Tenjin) Station|Nishitetsu Fukuoka (Tenjin) Station]] (South Exit) 1-minute walk * {{ill|Fukuoka City Subway|de|Fukuoka City Subway|ja|福岡市交通局|lt=Fukuoka City Subway|lt_ja=福岡市地下鉄|lt_de=Fukuoka City Subway|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka City Subway|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} [[:en:Kūkō Line (Fukuoka City Subway)|Airport Line]] [[:en:Tenjin Station|Tenjin Station]] 7-minute walk * Fukuoka City Subway [[:en:Nanakuma Line|Nanakuma Line]] [[:en:Tenjin-Minami Station|Tenjin-Minami Station]] 4-minute walk * {{ill|Nishitetsu Bus|de|Nishitetsu Bus|ja|西鉄バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitetsu Bus|qq=}} “Tenjin Kego Shrine – [[:en:Mitsukoshi|Mitsukoshi]] Front” 1-minute walk ==Photographs== File:Stone monument at Kego-jinja.JPG File:Stone animal monument at Kego-jinja.jpg ==External Links== {{Commonscat|Kego-jinja}} *[http://www.kegojinja.or.jp/ Kego Shrine] (official website) {{Shinto Side}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{translated page|ja|警固神社|version=103161857|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:警固神社]] [[de:Kego Shrine]] [[ja:%E8%AD%A6%E5%9B%BA%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:Kego Shrine]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to Empress Jingū]] [[Category:Tenjin, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka]] [[Category:Buildings in Chuo-ku, Fukuoka]] [[Category:History of Chūō Ward, Fukuoka City]] {{Commons category|Kego-jinja}}

In the 5th year of the [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō era]] ([[:en:1916|1916]]), it was Ranked as a Prefectural Shrine. The shrine’s name and the surrounding area “Kego” derive from the former government office “Kegosho” that was once located in the [[:en:Kōrokan|Kōrokukan]]. ==Facilities== [[File:Haiden of Kego Shrine 2.jpg|thumb|240px|[[:en:Haiden (Shinto)|Haiden]]]] [[File:Shimmon Gate of Kego Shrine.JPG|thumb|240px|{{ill|Shinmon Gate|de|Shinmon Gate|ja|神門|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmon Gate|qq=}}]] * Haiden (Worship Hall) * Honden (Main Hall) * Great Torii * Shinmon Gate * Harinoki Monument * Ima Masu Inari Shrine * Shintoku Hall (rental venue) {{ill|Kego Park|de|Kego Park|ja|警固公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kego Park|qq=}} borders the northern side of the shrine. ==Access== * {{ill|Yasomagatsuhinokami|de|Yasomagatsuhinokami|ja|禍津日神|lt=Yasomagatsuhinokami|lt_ja=八十禍津日神|lt_de=Yasomagatsuhinokami|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasomagatsuhinokami|qq=|1=Magatsuhinokami|comment=changed {{Coord|33.58761111111111|N|130.3996111111111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Magatsuhinokami|qid=Q11591203|1=Magatsuhi-no-kami}}; {{ill|Naobinokami|qid=Q11581794|1=Ōnaobi no Kami}} }} {{osm box|n|384542332}}
'''Kego Shrine''' (けごじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[:en:Fukuoka|Fukuoka City]] {{ill|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|de|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|ja|福岡市中央区 (福岡市)|lt=Chuo Ward|lt_ja=中央区|lt_de=Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} located at Tenjin 2-2-20, and is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shrine]]. Its former shrine Rank was {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|de|Präfektur-Schrein|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.
==Enshrined Deities== Kego Ōkami (the three deities): * {{ill|Yasomagatsuhinokami|de|Yasomagatsuhinokami|ja|禍津日神|lt=Yasomagatsuhinokami|lt_ja=八十禍津日神|lt_de=Yasomagatsuhinokami|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasomagatsuhinokami|qq=|1=Magatsuhinokami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591203|1=Magatsuhi-no-kami}} (yasomagatsuhinokami) * {{ill|Kannaobi no Kami|de|Kannaobi no Kami|ja|直毘神|lt=Kannaobi no Kami|lt_ja=神直毘神|lt_de=Kannaobi no Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannaobi no Kami|qq=|1=Naobinokami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581794|1=Ōnaobi no Kami}} (kannaobi no kami) * {{ill|Kannaobi no Kami|de|Kannaobi no Kami|ja|直毘神|lt=Great Kannaobi no Kami|lt_ja=大直毘神|lt_de=Great Kannaobi no Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannaobi no Kami|qq=|1=Naobinokami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581794|1=Ōnaobi no Kami}} (great kannaobi no kami)
==History== According to shrine legend, during the {{ill|Conquest of the Three Han|de|Eroberung der Drei Han|ja|三韓征伐|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Conquest of the Three Han|qq=|1=Silla–Wa War|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11249435}}, Empress Jingū’s fleet was protected and led to victory by Kego Ōkami, who was then enshrined at Fukasaki (around the area of the main bailey of [[:en:Fukuoka Castle|Fukuoka Castle]]), marking the beginning of the shrine. In the 6th year of the [[:en:Keichō|Keichō era]] ([[:en:1601|1601]]), during the construction of Fukuoka Castle, the shrine was moved to Shimokego Village (in the area around present-day [[:en:Fukuoka|Fukuoka City]], {{ill|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|de|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|ja|福岡市中央区 (福岡市)|lt=Chuo Ward|lt_ja=中央区|lt_de=Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Tenjin (kami)|Tenjin]])). In the 13th year of the [[:en:Keichō|Keichō era]] ([[:en:1608|1608]]), [[:en:Kuroda Nagamasa|Kuroda Nagamasa]], the lord of Fukuoka Castle, had a shrine building erected at its current location. In the 5th year of the [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō era]] ([[:en:1916|1916]]), it was Ranked as a Prefectural Shrine.
The shrine’s name and the surrounding area “Kego” derive from the former government office “Kegosho” that was once located in the [[:en:Kōrokan|Kōrokukan]].
==Facilities== [[File:Haiden of Kego Shrine 2.jpg|thumb|240px|[[:en:Haiden (Shinto)|Haiden]]]] [[File:Shimmon Gate of Kego Shrine.JPG|thumb|240px|{{ill|Shinmon Gate|de|Shinmon Gate|ja|神門|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmon Gate|qq=|1=Shinmon Gate|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591017}}]] * Haiden (Worship Hall) * Honden (Main Hall) * Great Torii * Shinmon Gate * Harinoki Monument * Ima Masu Inari Shrine * Shintoku Hall (rental venue)
{{ill|Kego Park|de|Kego Park|ja|警固公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kego Park|qq=|1=Kego Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632311}} borders the northern side of the shrine.
==Access== * [[:en:Nishi-Nippon Railroad|Nishi-Nippon Railroad]] [[:en:Nishitetsu Tenjin Ōmuta Line|Tenjin Omuta Line]] [[:en:Nishitetsu Fukuoka (Tenjin) Station|Nishitetsu Fukuoka (Tenjin) Station]] (South Exit) 1-minute walk * {{ill|Fukuoka City Subway|de|Fukuoka City Subway|ja|福岡市交通局|lt=Fukuoka City Subway|lt_ja=福岡市地下鉄|lt_de=Fukuoka City Subway|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka City Subway|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Fukuoka City Transportation Bureau|WD=Q27576993}} [[:en:Kūkō Line (Fukuoka City Subway)|Airport Line]] [[:en:Tenjin Station|Tenjin Station]] 7-minute walk * Fukuoka City Subway [[:en:Nanakuma Line|Nanakuma Line]] [[:en:Tenjin-Minami Station|Tenjin-Minami Station]] 4-minute walk * {{ill|Nishitetsu Bus|de|Nishitetsu Bus|ja|西鉄バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitetsu Bus|qq=|1=Nishitetsu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629843}} “Tenjin Kego Shrine – [[:en:Mitsukoshi|Mitsukoshi]] Front” 1-minute walk

'''Kego Shrine''' (けごじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[:en:Fukuoka|Fukuoka City]] {{ill|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|de|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|ja|福岡市中央区 (福岡市)|lt=Chuo Ward|lt_ja=中央区|lt_de=Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} located at Tenjin 2-2-20, and is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shrine]]. Its former shrine Rank was {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|de|Präfektur-Schrein|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.
==Enshrined Deities== Kego Ōkami (the three deities): * {{ill|Yasomagatsuhinokami|de|Yasomagatsuhinokami|ja|禍津日神|lt=Yasomagatsuhinokami|lt_ja=八十禍津日神|lt_de=Yasomagatsuhinokami|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasomagatsuhinokami|qq=|1=Magatsuhinokami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591203|1=Magatsuhi-no-kami}} (yasomagatsuhinokami) * {{ill|Kannaobi no Kami|de|Kannaobi no Kami|ja|直毘神|lt=Kannaobi no Kami|lt_ja=神直毘神|lt_de=Kannaobi no Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannaobi no Kami|qq=|1=Naobinokami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581794|1=Ōnaobi no Kami}} (kannaobi no kami) * {{ill|Kannaobi no Kami|de|Kannaobi no Kami|ja|直毘神|lt=Great Kannaobi no Kami|lt_ja=大直毘神|lt_de=Great Kannaobi no Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannaobi no Kami|qq=|1=Naobinokami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581794|1=Ōnaobi no Kami}} (great kannaobi no kami)
==History== According to shrine legend, during the {{ill|Conquest of the Three Han|de|Eroberung der Drei Han|ja|三韓征伐|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Conquest of the Three Han|qq=|1=Silla–Wa War|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11249435}}, Empress Jingū’s fleet was protected and led to victory by Kego Ōkami, who was then enshrined at Fukasaki (around the area of the main bailey of [[:en:Fukuoka Castle|Fukuoka Castle]]), marking the beginning of the shrine. In the 6th year of the [[:en:Keichō|Keichō era]] ([[:en:1601|1601]]), during the construction of Fukuoka Castle, the shrine was moved to Shimokego Village (in the area around present-day [[:en:Fukuoka|Fukuoka City]], {{ill|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|de|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|ja|福岡市中央区 (福岡市)|lt=Chuo Ward|lt_ja=中央区|lt_de=Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Tenjin (kami)|Tenjin]])). In the 13th year of the [[:en:Keichō|Keichō era]] ([[:en:1608|1608]]), [[:en:Kuroda Nagamasa|Kuroda Nagamasa]], the lord of Fukuoka Castle, had a shrine building erected at its current location. In the 5th year of the [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō era]] ([[:en:1916|1916]]), it was Ranked as a Prefectural Shrine.
The shrine’s name and the surrounding area “Kego” derive from the former government office “Kegosho” that was once located in the [[:en:Kōrokan|Kōrokukan]].
==Facilities== [[File:Haiden of Kego Shrine 2.jpg|thumb|240px|[[:en:Haiden (Shinto)|Haiden]]]] [[File:Shimmon Gate of Kego Shrine.JPG|thumb|240px|{{ill|Shinmon Gate|de|Shinmon Gate|ja|神門|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmon Gate|qq=|1=Shinmon Gate|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591017}}]] * Haiden (Worship Hall) * Honden (Main Hall) * Great Torii * Shinmon Gate * Harinoki Monument * Ima Masu Inari Shrine * Shintoku Hall (rental venue)
{{ill|Kego Park|de|Kego Park|ja|警固公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kego Park|qq=|1=Kego Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632311}} borders the northern side of the shrine.
==Access== * [[:en:Nishi-Nippon Railroad|Nishi-Nippon Railroad]] [[:en:Nishitetsu Tenjin Ōmuta Line|Tenjin Omuta Line]] [[:en:Nishitetsu Fukuoka (Tenjin) Station|Nishitetsu Fukuoka (Tenjin) Station]] (South Exit) 1-minute walk * {{ill|Fukuoka City Subway|de|Fukuoka City Subway|ja|福岡市交通局|lt=Fukuoka City Subway|lt_ja=福岡市地下鉄|lt_de=Fukuoka City Subway|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka City Subway|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Fukuoka City Transportation Bureau|WD=Q27576993}} [[:en:Kūkō Line (Fukuoka City Subway)|Airport Line]] [[:en:Tenjin Station|Tenjin Station]] 7-minute walk * Fukuoka City Subway [[:en:Nanakuma Line|Nanakuma Line]] [[:en:Tenjin-Minami Station|Tenjin-Minami Station]] 4-minute walk * {{ill|Nishitetsu Bus|de|Nishitetsu Bus|ja|西鉄バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitetsu Bus|qq=|1=Nishitetsu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629843}} “Tenjin Kego Shrine – [[:en:Mitsukoshi|Mitsukoshi]] Front” 1-minute walk

'''Kego Shrine''' (けごじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[:en:Fukuoka|Fukuoka City]] {{ill|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|de|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|ja|福岡市中央区 (福岡市)|lt=Chuo Ward|lt_ja=中央区|lt_de=Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} located at Tenjin 2-2-20, and is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shrine]]. Its former shrine Rank was {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|de|Präfektur-Schrein|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.
==Enshrined Deities== Kego Ōkami (the three deities): * {{ill|Yasomagatsuhinokami|de|Yasomagatsuhinokami|ja|禍津日神|lt=Yasomagatsuhinokami|lt_ja=八十禍津日神|lt_de=Yasomagatsuhinokami|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasomagatsuhinokami|1=Magatsuhinokami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591203|1=Magatsuhi-no-kami}} (yasomagatsuhinokami) * {{ill|Kannaobi no Kami|de|Kannaobi no Kami|ja|直毘神|lt=Kannaobi no Kami|lt_ja=神直毘神|lt_de=Kannaobi no Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannaobi no Kami|1=Naobinokami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581794|1=Ōnaobi no Kami}} (kannaobi no kami) * {{ill|Kannaobi no Kami|de|Kannaobi no Kami|ja|直毘神|lt=Great Kannaobi no Kami|lt_ja=大直毘神|lt_de=Great Kannaobi no Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannaobi no Kami|1=Naobinokami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581794|1=Ōnaobi no Kami}} (great kannaobi no kami)
==History== According to shrine legend, during the {{ill|Conquest of the Three Han|de|Eroberung der Drei Han|ja|三韓征伐|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Conquest of the Three Han|1=Silla–Wa War|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11249435}}, Empress Jingū’s fleet was protected and led to victory by Kego Ōkami, who was then enshrined at Fukasaki (around the area of the main bailey of [[:en:Fukuoka Castle|Fukuoka Castle]]), marking the beginning of the shrine. In the 6th year of the [[:en:Keichō|Keichō era]] ([[:en:1601|1601]]), during the construction of Fukuoka Castle, the shrine was moved to Shimokego Village (in the area around present-day [[:en:Fukuoka|Fukuoka City]], {{ill|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|de|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|ja|福岡市中央区 (福岡市)|lt=Chuo Ward|lt_ja=中央区|lt_de=Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Tenjin (kami)|Tenjin]])). In the 13th year of the [[:en:Keichō|Keichō era]] ([[:en:1608|1608]]), [[:en:Kuroda Nagamasa|Kuroda Nagamasa]], the lord of Fukuoka Castle, had a shrine building erected at its current location. In the 5th year of the [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō era]] ([[:en:1916|1916]]), it was Ranked as a Prefectural Shrine.
The shrine’s name and the surrounding area “Kego” derive from the former government office “Kegosho” that was once located in the [[:en:Kōrokan|Kōrokukan]].
==Facilities== [[File:Haiden of Kego Shrine 2.jpg|thumb|240px|[[:en:Haiden (Shinto)|Haiden]]]] [[File:Shimmon Gate of Kego Shrine.JPG|thumb|240px|{{ill|Shinmon Gate|de|Shinmon Gate|ja|神門|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmon Gate|1=Shinmon Gate|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591017}}]] * Haiden (Worship Hall) * Honden (Main Hall) * Great Torii * Shinmon Gate * Harinoki Monument * Ima Masu Inari Shrine * Shintoku Hall (rental venue)
{{ill|Kego Park|de|Kego Park|ja|警固公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kego Park|1=Kego Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632311}} borders the northern side of the shrine.
==Access== * [[:en:Nishi-Nippon Railroad|Nishi-Nippon Railroad]] [[:en:Nishitetsu Tenjin Ōmuta Line|Tenjin Omuta Line]] [[:en:Nishitetsu Fukuoka (Tenjin) Station|Nishitetsu Fukuoka (Tenjin) Station]] (South Exit) 1-minute walk * {{ill|Fukuoka City Subway|de|Fukuoka City Subway|ja|福岡市交通局|lt=Fukuoka City Subway|lt_ja=福岡市地下鉄|lt_de=Fukuoka City Subway|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka City Subway|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Fukuoka City Transportation Bureau|WD=Q27576993}} [[:en:Kūkō Line (Fukuoka City Subway)|Airport Line]] [[:en:Tenjin Station|Tenjin Station]] 7-minute walk * Fukuoka City Subway [[:en:Nanakuma Line|Nanakuma Line]] [[:en:Tenjin-Minami Station|Tenjin-Minami Station]] 4-minute walk * {{ill|Nishitetsu Bus|de|Nishitetsu Bus|ja|西鉄バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitetsu Bus|1=Nishitetsu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629843}} “Tenjin Kego Shrine – [[:en:Mitsukoshi|Mitsukoshi]] Front” 1-minute walk

==History== According to shrine legend, during the {{ill|Conquest of the Three Han|de|Eroberung der Drei Han|ja|三韓征伐|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Conquest of the Three Han|1=Silla–Wa War|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11249435}}, Empress Jingū’s fleet was protected and led to victory by Kego Ōkami, who was then enshrined at Fukasaki (around the area of the main bailey of [[:en:Fukuoka Castle|Fukuoka Castle]]), marking the beginning of the shrine. In the 6th year of the [[:en:Keichō|Keichō era]] ([[:en:1601|1601]]), during the construction of Fukuoka Castle, the shrine was moved to Shimokego Village (in the area around present-day [[:en:Fukuoka|Fukuoka City]], {{ill|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|de|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|ja|福岡市中央区 (福岡市)|lt=Chuo Ward|lt_ja=中央区|lt_de=Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Tenjin (kami)|Tenjin]])). In the 13th year of the [[:en:Keichō|Keichō era]] ([[:en:1608|1608]]), [[:en:Kuroda Nagamasa|Kuroda Nagamasa]], the lord of Fukuoka Castle, had a shrine building erected at its current location. In the 5th year of the [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō era]] ([[:en:1916|1916]]), it was Ranked as a Prefectural Shrine.
The shrine’s name and the surrounding area “Kego” derive from the former government office “Kegosho” that was once located in the [[:en:Kōrokan|Kōrokukan]].

{{ill|Kego Park|de|Kego Park|ja|警固公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kego Park|1=Kego Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632311}} borders the northern side of the shrine.

'''Kego Shrine''' (けごじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[:en:Fukuoka|Fukuoka City]] {{ill|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|de|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|ja|福岡市中央区 (福岡市)|lt=Chuo Ward|lt_ja=中央区|lt_de=Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} located at Tenjin 2-2-20, and is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shrine]]. Its former shrine Rank was {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|de|Präfektur-Schrein|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.
==Enshrined Deities== Kego Ōkami (the three deities): * {{ill|Yasomagatsuhinokami|de|Yasomagatsuhinokami|ja|禍津日神|lt=Yasomagatsuhinokami|lt_ja=八十禍津日神|lt_de=Yasomagatsuhinokami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasomagatsuhinokami|1=Magatsuhinokami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591203|1=Magatsuhi-no-kami}} (yasomagatsuhinokami) * {{ill|Kannaobi no Kami|de|Kannaobi no Kami|ja|直毘神|lt=Kannaobi no Kami|lt_ja=神直毘神|lt_de=Kannaobi no Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannaobi no Kami|1=Naobinokami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581794|1=Ōnaobi no Kami}} (kannaobi no kami) * {{ill|Kannaobi no Kami|de|Kannaobi no Kami|ja|直毘神|lt=Great Kannaobi no Kami|lt_ja=大直毘神|lt_de=Great Kannaobi no Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannaobi no Kami|1=Naobinokami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581794|1=Ōnaobi no Kami}} (great kannaobi no kami)
==History== According to shrine legend, during the {{ill|Conquest of the Three Han|de|Eroberung der Drei Han|ja|三韓征伐|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Conquest of the Three Han|1=Silla–Wa War|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11249435}}, Empress Jingū’s fleet was protected and led to victory by Kego Ōkami, who was then enshrined at Fukasaki (around the area of the main bailey of [[:en:Fukuoka Castle|Fukuoka Castle]]), marking the beginning of the shrine. In the 6th year of the [[:en:Keichō|Keichō era]] ([[:en:1601|1601]]), during the construction of Fukuoka Castle, the shrine was moved to Shimokego Village (in the area around present-day [[:en:Fukuoka|Fukuoka City]], {{ill|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|de|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|ja|福岡市中央区 (福岡市)|lt=Chuo Ward|lt_ja=中央区|lt_de=Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Tenjin (kami)|Tenjin]])). In the 13th year of the [[:en:Keichō|Keichō era]] ([[:en:1608|1608]]), [[:en:Kuroda Nagamasa|Kuroda Nagamasa]], the lord of Fukuoka Castle, had a shrine building erected at its current location. In the 5th year of the [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō era]] ([[:en:1916|1916]]), it was Ranked as a Prefectural Shrine.
The shrine’s name and the surrounding area “Kego” derive from the former government office “Kegosho” that was once located in the [[:en:Kōrokan|Kōrokukan]].
==Facilities== [[File:Haiden of Kego Shrine 2.jpg|thumb|240px|[[:en:Haiden (Shinto)|Haiden]]]] [[File:Shimmon Gate of Kego Shrine.JPG|thumb|240px|{{ill|Shinmon Gate|de|Shinmon Gate|ja|神門|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmon Gate|1=Shinmon Gate|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591017}}]] * Haiden (Worship Hall) * Honden (Main Hall) * Great Torii * Shinmon Gate * Harinoki Monument * Ima Masu Inari Shrine * Shintoku Hall (rental venue)
{{ill|Kego Park|de|Kego Park|ja|警固公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kego Park|1=Kego Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632311}} borders the northern side of the shrine.
==Access== * [[:en:Nishi-Nippon Railroad|Nishi-Nippon Railroad]] [[:en:Nishitetsu Tenjin Ōmuta Line|Tenjin Omuta Line]] [[:en:Nishitetsu Fukuoka (Tenjin) Station|Nishitetsu Fukuoka (Tenjin) Station]] (South Exit) 1-minute walk * {{ill|Fukuoka City Subway|de|Fukuoka City Subway|ja|福岡市交通局|lt=Fukuoka City Subway|lt_ja=福岡市地下鉄|lt_de=Fukuoka City Subway|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka City Subway|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Fukuoka City Transportation Bureau|WD=Q27576993}} [[:en:Kūkō Line (Fukuoka City Subway)|Airport Line]] [[:en:Tenjin Station|Tenjin Station]] 7-minute walk * Fukuoka City Subway [[:en:Nanakuma Line|Nanakuma Line]] [[:en:Tenjin-Minami Station|Tenjin-Minami Station]] 4-minute walk * {{ill|Nishitetsu Bus|de|Nishitetsu Bus|ja|西鉄バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitetsu Bus|1=Nishitetsu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629843}} “Tenjin Kego Shrine – [[:en:Mitsukoshi|Mitsukoshi]] Front” 1-minute walk

Kami|de|Kannaobi no Kami|ja|神直毘神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannaobi no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神直日神|1=Kamuna'obi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q86725467}} ・ {{ill|Kannaobi no Kami|de|Kannaobi no Kami|ja|直毘神|lt=Great Kannaobi no Kami|lt_ja=大直毘神|lt_de=Kannaobi no Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannaobi no Kami|1=Naobinokami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581794|1=Ōnaobi no Kami}} ・ {{ill|Yasomagatsuhinokami|de|Yasomagatsuhinokami|ja|禍津日神|lt=Yasomagatsuhinokami|lt_ja=八十禍津日神|lt_de=Yasomagatsuhinokami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasomagatsuhinokami|1=Magatsuhinokami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591203|1=Magatsuhi-no-kami}}) |Founded = [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]] 9th year |Status = Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|de|Präfektur-Schrein|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Festival = Saturdays and Sundays after [[:en:July 19|July 19]] (Natsugoshi Festival) |location = {{ill|Tenjin|WD=Q603281}} |coordinates = {{Coord|33.58761111111111|N|130.3996111111111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Magatsuhinokami|WD=Q11591203|1=Magatsuhi-no-kami}}; {{ill|Naobinokami|WD=Q11581794|1=Ōnaobi no Kami}} }} {{osm box|n|384542332}}
'''Kego Shrine''' (けごじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[:en:Fukuoka|Fukuoka City]] {{ill|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|de|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|ja|福岡市中央区 (福岡市)|lt=Chuo Ward|lt_ja=中央区|lt_de=Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} located at Tenjin 2-2-20, and is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shrine]]. Its former shrine Rank was {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|de|Präfektur-Schrein|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.
==Enshrined Deities== Kego Ōkami (the three deities): * {{ill|Yasomagatsuhinokami|de|Yasomagatsuhinokami|ja|禍津日神|lt=Yasomagatsuhinokami|lt_ja=八十禍津日神|lt_de=Yasomagatsuhinokami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasomagatsuhinokami|1=Magatsuhinokami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591203|1=Magatsuhi-no-kami}} (yasomagatsuhinokami) * {{ill|Kannaobi no Kami|de|Kannaobi no Kami|ja|直毘神|lt=Kannaobi no Kami|lt_ja=神直毘神|lt_de=Kannaobi no Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannaobi no Kami|1=Naobinokami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581794|1=Ōnaobi no Kami}} (kannaobi no kami) * {{ill|Kannaobi no Kami|de|Kannaobi no Kami|ja|直毘神|lt=Great Kannaobi no Kami|lt_ja=大直毘神|lt_de=Great Kannaobi no Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannaobi no Kami|1=Naobinokami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581794|1=Ōnaobi no Kami}} (great kannaobi no kami)
==History== According to shrine legend, during the {{ill|Conquest of the Three Han|de|Eroberung der Drei Han|ja|三韓征伐|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Conquest of the Three Han|1=Silla–Wa War|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11249435}}, Empress Jingū’s fleet was protected and led to victory by Kego Ōkami, who was then enshrined at Fukasaki (around the area of the main bailey of [[:en:Fukuoka Castle|Fukuoka Castle]]), marking the beginning of the shrine. In the 6th year of the [[:en:Keichō|Keichō era]] ([[:en:1601|1601]]), during the construction of Fukuoka Castle, the shrine was moved to Shimokego Village (in the area around present-day [[:en:Fukuoka|Fukuoka City]], {{ill|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|de|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|ja|福岡市中央区 (福岡市)|lt=Chuo Ward|lt_ja=中央区|lt_de=Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Tenjin (kami)|Tenjin]])). In the 13th year of the [[:en:Keichō|Keichō era]] ([[:en:1608|1608]]), [[:en:Kuroda Nagamasa|Kuroda Nagamasa]], the lord of Fukuoka Castle, had a shrine building erected at its current location. In the 5th year of the [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō era]] ([[:en:1916|1916]]), it was Ranked as a Prefectural Shrine.
The shrine’s name and the surrounding area “Kego” derive from the former government office “Kegosho” that was once located in the [[:en:Kōrokan|Kōrokukan]].
==Facilities== [[File:Haiden of Kego Shrine 2.jpg|thumb|240px|[[:en:Haiden (Shinto)|Haiden]]]] [[File:Shimmon Gate of Kego Shrine.JPG|thumb|240px|{{ill|Shinmon Gate|de|Shinmon Gate|ja|神門|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmon Gate|1=Shinmon Gate|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591017}}]] * Haiden (Worship Hall) * Honden (Main Hall) * Great Torii * Shinmon Gate * Harinoki Monument * Ima Masu Inari Shrine * Shintoku Hall (rental venue)
{{ill|Kego Park|de|Kego Park|ja|警固公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kego Park|1=Kego Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632311}} borders the northern side of the shrine.
==Access== * [[:en:Nishi-Nippon Railroad|Nishi-Nippon Railroad]] [[:en:Nishitetsu Tenjin Ōmuta Line|Tenjin Omuta Line]] [[:en:Nishitetsu Fukuoka (Tenjin) Station|Nishitetsu Fukuoka (Tenjin) Station]] (South Exit) 1-minute walk * {{ill|Fukuoka City Subway|de|Fukuoka City Subway|ja|福岡市交通局|lt=Fukuoka City Subway|lt_ja=福岡市地下鉄|lt_de=Fukuoka City Subway|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka City Subway|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Fukuoka City Transportation Bureau|WD=Q27576993}} [[:en:Kūkō Line (Fukuoka City Subway)|Airport Line]] [[:en:Tenjin Station|Tenjin Station]] 7-minute walk * Fukuoka City Subway [[:en:Nanakuma Line|Nanakuma Line]] [[:en:Tenjin-Minami Station|Tenjin-Minami Station]] 4-minute walk * {{ill|Nishitetsu Bus|de|Nishitetsu Bus|ja|西鉄バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitetsu Bus|1=Nishitetsu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629843}} “Tenjin Kego Shrine – [[:en:Mitsukoshi|Mitsukoshi]] Front” 1-minute walk
==Photographs== <Gallery> File:Stone monument at Kego-jinja.JPG File:Stone animal monument at Kego-jinja.jpg </Gallery>
==External Links== {{Commonscat|Kego-jinja}}

'''Kego Shrine''' (けごじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[:en:Fukuoka|Fukuoka City]] {{ill|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|de|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|ja|福岡市中央区 (福岡市)|lt=Chuo Ward|lt_ja=中央区|lt_de=Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} located at Tenjin 2-2-20, and is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shrine]]. Its former shrine Rank was {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|de|Präfektur-Schrein|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.
==Enshrined Deities== Kego Ōkami (the three deities): * {{ill|Yasomagatsuhinokami|de|Yasomagatsuhinokami|ja|禍津日神|lt=Yasomagatsuhinokami|lt_ja=八十禍津日神|lt_de=Yasomagatsuhinokami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasomagatsuhinokami|1=Magatsuhinokami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591203|1=Magatsuhi-no-kami}} (yasomagatsuhinokami) * {{ill|Kannaobi no Kami|de|Kannaobi no Kami|ja|直毘神|lt=Kannaobi no Kami|lt_ja=神直毘神|lt_de=Kannaobi no Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannaobi no Kami|1=Naobinokami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581794|1=Ōnaobi no Kami}} (kannaobi no kami) * {{ill|Kannaobi no Kami|de|Kannaobi no Kami|ja|直毘神|lt=Great Kannaobi no Kami|lt_ja=大直毘神|lt_de=Great Kannaobi no Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannaobi no Kami|1=Naobinokami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581794|1=Ōnaobi no Kami}} (great kannaobi no kami)
==History== According to shrine legend, during the {{ill|Conquest of the Three Han|de|Eroberung der Drei Han|ja|三韓征伐|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Conquest of the Three Han|1=Silla–Wa War|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11249435}}, Empress Jingū’s fleet was protected and led to victory by Kego Ōkami, who was then enshrined at Fukasaki (around the area of the main bailey of [[:en:Fukuoka Castle|Fukuoka Castle]]), marking the beginning of the shrine. In the 6th year of the [[:en:Keichō|Keichō era]] ([[:en:1601|1601]]), during the construction of Fukuoka Castle, the shrine was moved to Shimokego Village (in the area around present-day [[:en:Fukuoka|Fukuoka City]], {{ill|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|de|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|ja|福岡市中央区 (福岡市)|lt=Chuo Ward|lt_ja=中央区|lt_de=Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Tenjin (kami)|Tenjin]])). In the 13th year of the [[:en:Keichō|Keichō era]] ([[:en:1608|1608]]), [[:en:Kuroda Nagamasa|Kuroda Nagamasa]], the lord of Fukuoka Castle, had a shrine building erected at its current location. In the 5th year of the [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō era]] ([[:en:1916|1916]]), it was Ranked as a Prefectural Shrine.
The shrine’s name and the surrounding area “Kego” derive from the former government office “Kegosho” that was once located in the [[:en:Kōrokan|Kōrokukan]].
==Facilities== [[File:Haiden of Kego Shrine 2.jpg|thumb|240px|[[:en:Haiden (Shinto)|Haiden]]]] [[File:Shimmon Gate of Kego Shrine.JPG|thumb|240px|{{ill|Shinmon Gate|de|Shinmon Gate|ja|神門|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmon Gate|1=Shinmon Gate|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591017}}]] * Haiden (Worship Hall) * Honden (Main Hall) * Great Torii * Shinmon Gate * Harinoki Monument * Ima Masu Inari Shrine * Shintoku Hall (rental venue)
{{ill|Kego Park|de|Kego Park|ja|警固公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kego Park|1=Kego Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632311}} borders the northern side of the shrine.
==Access== * [[:en:Nishi-Nippon Railroad|Nishi-Nippon Railroad]] [[:en:Nishitetsu Tenjin Ōmuta Line|Tenjin Omuta Line]] [[:en:Nishitetsu Fukuoka (Tenjin) Station|Nishitetsu Fukuoka (Tenjin) Station]] (South Exit) 1-minute walk * {{ill|Fukuoka City Subway|de|Fukuoka City Subway|ja|福岡市交通局|lt=Fukuoka City Subway|lt_ja=福岡市地下鉄|lt_de=Fukuoka City Subway|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka City Subway|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Fukuoka City Transportation Bureau|WD=Q27576993}} [[:en:Kūkō Line (Fukuoka City Subway)|Airport Line]] [[:en:Tenjin Station|Tenjin Station]] 7-minute walk * Fukuoka City Subway [[:en:Nanakuma Line|Nanakuma Line]] [[:en:Tenjin-Minami Station|Tenjin-Minami Station]] 4-minute walk * {{ill|Nishitetsu Bus|de|Nishitetsu Bus|ja|西鉄バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitetsu Bus|1=Nishitetsu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629843}} “Tenjin Kego Shrine – [[:en:Mitsukoshi|Mitsukoshi]] Front” 1-minute walk

{{ill|Kego Park|de|Kego Park|ja|警固公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kego Park|1=Kego Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632311}} borders the northern side of the shrine.
==Access== * [[:en:Nishi-Nippon Railroad|Nishi-Nippon Railroad]] [[:en:Nishitetsu Tenjin Ōmuta Line|Tenjin Omuta Line]] [[:en:Nishitetsu Fukuoka (Tenjin) Station|Nishitetsu Fukuoka (Tenjin) Station]] (South Exit) 1-minute walk * {{ill|Fukuoka City Subway|de|Fukuoka City Subway|ja|福岡市交通局|lt=Fukuoka City Subway|lt_ja=福岡市地下鉄|lt_de=Fukuoka City Subway|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka City Subway|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Fukuoka City Transportation Bureau|WD=Q27576993}} [[:en:Kūkō Line (Fukuoka City Subway)|Airport Line]] [[:en:Tenjin Station|Tenjin Station]] 7-minute walk * Fukuoka City Subway [[:en:Nanakuma Line|Nanakuma Line]] [[:en:Tenjin-Minami Station|Tenjin-Minami Station]] 4-minute walk * {{ill|Nishitetsu Bus|de|Nishitetsu Bus|ja|西鉄バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitetsu Bus|1=Nishitetsu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629843}} “Tenjin Kego Shrine – [[:en:Mitsukoshi|Mitsukoshi]] Front” 1-minute walk

no Kami|ja|直毘神|lt=Great Kannaobi no Kami|lt_ja=大直毘神|lt_de=Kannaobi no Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannaobi no Kami|1=Naobinokami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581794|1=Ōnaobi no {{ill|Yasomagatsuhinokami|de|Yasomagatsuhinokami|ja|禍津日神|lt=Yasomagatsuhinokami|lt_ja=八十禍津日神|lt_de=Yasomagatsuhinokami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasomagatsuhinokami|1=Magatsuhinokami|comment=changed Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Festival = Saturdays and Sundays after [[:en:July 19|July 19]] (Natsugoshi Festival) |location = {{ill|Tenjin|WD=Q603281}} |coordinates = {{Coord|33.58761111111111|N|130.3996111111111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Magatsuhinokami|WD=Q11591203|1=Magatsuhi-no-kami}}; {{ill|Naobinokami|WD=Q11581794|1=Ōnaobi no Kami}} }} {{osm box|n|384542332}}
'''Kego Shrine''' (けごじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[:en:Fukuoka|Fukuoka City]] {{ill|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|de|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|ja|福岡市中央区 (福岡市)|lt=Chuo Ward|lt_ja=中央区|lt_de=Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} located at Tenjin 2-2-20, and is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shrine]]. Its former shrine Rank was {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|de|Präfektur-Schrein|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.
==Enshrined Deities== Kego Ōkami (the three deities): * {{ill|Yasomagatsuhinokami|de|Yasomagatsuhinokami|ja|禍津日神|lt=Yasomagatsuhinokami|lt_ja=八十禍津日神|lt_de=Yasomagatsuhinokami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasomagatsuhinokami|1=Magatsuhinokami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591203|1=Magatsuhi-no-kami}} (yasomagatsuhinokami) * {{ill|Kannaobi no Kami|de|Kannaobi no Kami|ja|直毘神|lt=Kannaobi no Kami|lt_ja=神直毘神|lt_de=Kannaobi no Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannaobi no Kami|1=Naobinokami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581794|1=Ōnaobi no Kami}} (kannaobi no kami) * {{ill|Kannaobi no Kami|de|Kannaobi no Kami|ja|直毘神|lt=Great Kannaobi no Kami|lt_ja=大直毘神|lt_de=Great Kannaobi no Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannaobi no Kami|1=Naobinokami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581794|1=Ōnaobi no Kami}} (great kannaobi no kami)
==History== According to shrine legend, during the {{ill|Conquest of the Three Han|de|Eroberung der Drei Han|ja|三韓征伐|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Conquest of the Three Han|1=Silla–Wa War|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11249435}}, Empress Jingū’s fleet was protected and led to victory by Kego Ōkami, who was then enshrined at Fukasaki (around the area of the main bailey of [[:en:Fukuoka Castle|Fukuoka Castle]]), marking the beginning of the shrine. In the 6th year of the [[:en:Keichō|Keichō era]] ([[:en:1601|1601]]), during the construction of Fukuoka Castle, the shrine was moved to Shimokego Village (in the area around present-day [[:en:Fukuoka|Fukuoka City]], {{ill|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|de|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|ja|福岡市中央区 (福岡市)|lt=Chuo Ward|lt_ja=中央区|lt_de=Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Tenjin (kami)|Tenjin]])). In the 13th year of the [[:en:Keichō|Keichō era]] ([[:en:1608|1608]]), [[:en:Kuroda Nagamasa|Kuroda Nagamasa]], the lord of Fukuoka Castle, had a shrine building erected at its current location. In the 5th year of the [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō era]] ([[:en:1916|1916]]), it was Ranked as a Prefectural Shrine.
The shrine’s name and the surrounding area “Kego” derive from the former government office “Kegosho” that was once located in the [[:en:Kōrokan|Kōrokukan]].
==Facilities== [[File:Haiden of Kego Shrine 2.jpg|thumb|240px|[[:en:Haiden (Shinto)|Haiden]]]] [[File:Shimmon Gate of Kego Shrine.JPG|thumb|240px|{{ill|Shinmon Gate|de|Shinmon Gate|ja|神門|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmon Gate|1=Shinmon Gate|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591017}}]] * Haiden (Worship Hall) * Honden (Main Hall) * Great Torii * Shinmon Gate * Harinoki Monument * Ima Masu Inari Shrine * Shintoku Hall (rental venue)
{{ill|Kego Park|de|Kego Park|ja|警固公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kego Park|1=Kego Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632311}} borders the northern side of the shrine.
==Access== * [[:en:Nishi-Nippon Railroad|Nishi-Nippon Railroad]] [[:en:Nishitetsu Tenjin Ōmuta Line|Tenjin Omuta Line]] [[:en:Nishitetsu Fukuoka (Tenjin) Station|Nishitetsu Fukuoka (Tenjin) Station]] (South Exit) 1-minute walk * {{ill|Fukuoka City Subway|de|Fukuoka City Subway|ja|福岡市交通局|lt=Fukuoka City Subway|lt_ja=福岡市地下鉄|lt_de=Fukuoka City Subway|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka City Subway|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Fukuoka City Transportation Bureau|WD=Q27576993}} [[:en:Kūkō Line (Fukuoka City Subway)|Airport Line]] [[:en:Tenjin Station|Tenjin Station]] 7-minute walk * Fukuoka City Subway [[:en:Nanakuma Line|Nanakuma Line]] [[:en:Tenjin-Minami Station|Tenjin-Minami Station]] 4-minute walk * {{ill|Nishitetsu Bus|de|Nishitetsu Bus|ja|西鉄バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitetsu Bus|1=Nishitetsu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629843}} “Tenjin Kego Shrine – [[:en:Mitsukoshi|Mitsukoshi]] Front” 1-minute walk

'''Kego Shrine''' (けごじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[:en:Fukuoka|Fukuoka City]] {{ill|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|de|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|ja|福岡市中央区 (福岡市)|lt=Chuo Ward|lt_ja=中央区|lt_de=Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} located at Tenjin 2-2-20, and is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shrine]]. Its former shrine Rank was {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|de|Präfektur-Schrein|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.
==Enshrined Deities== Kego Ōkami (the three deities): * {{ill|Yasomagatsuhinokami|de|Yasomagatsuhinokami|ja|禍津日神|lt=Yasomagatsuhinokami|lt_ja=八十禍津日神|lt_de=Yasomagatsuhinokami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasomagatsuhinokami|1=Magatsuhinokami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591203|1=Magatsuhi-no-kami}} (yasomagatsuhinokami) * {{ill|Kannaobi no Kami|de|Kannaobi no Kami|ja|直毘神|lt=Kannaobi no Kami|lt_ja=神直毘神|lt_de=Kannaobi no Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannaobi no Kami|1=Naobinokami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581794|1=Ōnaobi no Kami}} (kannaobi no kami) * {{ill|Kannaobi no Kami|de|Kannaobi no Kami|ja|直毘神|lt=Great Kannaobi no Kami|lt_ja=大直毘神|lt_de=Great Kannaobi no Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannaobi no Kami|1=Naobinokami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581794|1=Ōnaobi no Kami}} (great kannaobi no kami)
==History== According to shrine legend, during the {{ill|Conquest of the Three Han|de|Eroberung der Drei Han|ja|三韓征伐|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Conquest of the Three Han|1=Silla–Wa War|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11249435}}, Empress Jingū’s fleet was protected and led to victory by Kego Ōkami, who was then enshrined at Fukasaki (around the area of the main bailey of [[:en:Fukuoka Castle|Fukuoka Castle]]), marking the beginning of the shrine. In the 6th year of the [[:en:Keichō|Keichō era]] ([[:en:1601|1601]]), during the construction of Fukuoka Castle, the shrine was moved to Shimokego Village (in the area around present-day [[:en:Fukuoka|Fukuoka City]], {{ill|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|de|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|ja|福岡市中央区 (福岡市)|lt=Chuo Ward|lt_ja=中央区|lt_de=Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Tenjin (kami)|Tenjin]])). In the 13th year of the [[:en:Keichō|Keichō era]] ([[:en:1608|1608]]), [[:en:Kuroda Nagamasa|Kuroda Nagamasa]], the lord of Fukuoka Castle, had a shrine building erected at its current location. In the 5th year of the [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō era]] ([[:en:1916|1916]]), it was Ranked as a Prefectural Shrine.
The shrine’s name and the surrounding area “Kego” derive from the former government office “Kegosho” that was once located in the [[:en:Kōrokan|Kōrokukan]].
==Facilities== [[File:Haiden of Kego Shrine 2.jpg|thumb|240px|[[:en:Haiden (Shinto)|Haiden]]]] [[File:Shimmon Gate of Kego Shrine.JPG|thumb|240px|{{ill|Shinmon Gate|de|Shinmon Gate|ja|神門|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmon Gate|1=Shinmon Gate|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591017}}]] * Haiden (Worship Hall) * Honden (Main Hall) * Great Torii * Shinmon Gate * Harinoki Monument * Ima Masu Inari Shrine * Shintoku Hall (rental venue)
{{ill|Kego Park|de|Kego Park|ja|警固公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kego Park|1=Kego Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632311}} borders the northern side of the shrine.
==Access== * [[:en:Nishi-Nippon Railroad|Nishi-Nippon Railroad]] [[:en:Nishitetsu Tenjin Ōmuta Line|Tenjin Omuta Line]] [[:en:Nishitetsu Fukuoka (Tenjin) Station|Nishitetsu Fukuoka (Tenjin) Station]] (South Exit) 1-minute walk * {{ill|Fukuoka City Subway|de|Fukuoka City Subway|ja|福岡市交通局|lt=Fukuoka City Subway|lt_ja=福岡市地下鉄|lt_de=Fukuoka City Subway|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka City Subway|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Fukuoka City Transportation Bureau|WD=Q27576993}} [[:en:Kūkō Line (Fukuoka City Subway)|Airport Line]] [[:en:Tenjin Station|Tenjin Station]] 7-minute walk * Fukuoka City Subway [[:en:Nanakuma Line|Nanakuma Line]] [[:en:Tenjin-Minami Station|Tenjin-Minami Station]] 4-minute walk * {{ill|Nishitetsu Bus|de|Nishitetsu Bus|ja|西鉄バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitetsu Bus|1=Nishitetsu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629843}} “Tenjin Kego Shrine – [[:en:Mitsukoshi|Mitsukoshi]] Front” 1-minute walk

==Enshrined Deities== Kego Ōkami (the three deities): * {{ill|Yasomagatsuhinokami|de|Yasomagatsuhinokami|ja|禍津日神|lt=Yasomagatsuhinokami|lt_ja=八十禍津日神|lt_de=Yasomagatsuhinokami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasomagatsuhinokami|1=Magatsuhinokami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591203|1=Magatsuhi-no-kami}} (yasomagatsuhinokami) * {{ill|Kannaobi no Kami|de|Kannaobi no Kami|ja|直毘神|lt=Kannaobi no Kami|lt_ja=神直毘神|lt_de=Kannaobi no Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannaobi no Kami|1=Naobinokami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581794|1=Ōnaobi no Kami}} (kannaobi no kami) * {{ill|Kannaobi no Kami|de|Kannaobi no Kami|ja|直毘神|lt=Great Kannaobi no Kami|lt_ja=大直毘神|lt_de=Great Kannaobi no Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannaobi no Kami|1=Naobinokami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581794|1=Ōnaobi no Kami}} (great kannaobi no kami)
==History== According to shrine legend, during the {{ill|Conquest of the Three Han|de|Eroberung der Drei Han|ja|三韓征伐|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Conquest of the Three Han|1=Silla–Wa War|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11249435}}, Empress Jingū’s fleet was protected and led to victory by Kego Ōkami, who was then enshrined at Fukasaki (around the area of the main bailey of [[:en:Fukuoka Castle|Fukuoka Castle]]), marking the beginning of the shrine. In the 6th year of the [[:en:Keichō|Keichō era]] ([[:en:1601|1601]]), during the construction of Fukuoka Castle, the shrine was moved to Shimokego Village (in the area around present-day [[:en:Fukuoka|Fukuoka City]], {{ill|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|de|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|ja|福岡市中央区 (福岡市)|lt=Chuo Ward|lt_ja=中央区|lt_de=Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Tenjin (kami)|Tenjin]])). In the 13th year of the [[:en:Keichō|Keichō era]] ([[:en:1608|1608]]), [[:en:Kuroda Nagamasa|Kuroda Nagamasa]], the lord of Fukuoka Castle, had a shrine building erected at its current location. In the 5th year of the [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō era]] ([[:en:1916|1916]]), it was Ranked as a Prefectural Shrine.
The shrine’s name and the surrounding area “Kego” derive from the former government office “Kegosho” that was once located in the [[:en:Kōrokan|Kōrokukan]].
==Facilities== [[File:Haiden of Kego Shrine 2.jpg|thumb|240px|[[:en:Haiden (Shinto)|Haiden]]]] [[File:Shimmon Gate of Kego Shrine.JPG|thumb|240px|{{ill|Shinmon Gate|de|Shinmon Gate|ja|神門|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmon Gate|1=Shinmon Gate|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591017}}]] * Haiden (Worship Hall) * Honden (Main Hall) * Great Torii * Shinmon Gate * Harinoki Monument * Ima Masu Inari Shrine * Shintoku Hall (rental venue)
{{ill|Kego Park|de|Kego Park|ja|警固公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kego Park|1=Kego Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632311}} borders the northern side of the shrine.
==Access== * [[:en:Nishi-Nippon Railroad|Nishi-Nippon Railroad]] [[:en:Nishitetsu Tenjin Ōmuta Line|Tenjin Omuta Line]] [[:en:Nishitetsu Fukuoka (Tenjin) Station|Nishitetsu Fukuoka (Tenjin) Station]] (South Exit) 1-minute walk * {{ill|Fukuoka City Subway|de|Fukuoka City Subway|ja|福岡市交通局|lt=Fukuoka City Subway|lt_ja=福岡市地下鉄|lt_de=Fukuoka City Subway|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka City Subway|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Fukuoka City Transportation Bureau|WD=Q27576993}} [[:en:Kūkō Line (Fukuoka City Subway)|Airport Line]] [[:en:Tenjin Station|Tenjin Station]] 7-minute walk * Fukuoka City Subway [[:en:Nanakuma Line|Nanakuma Line]] [[:en:Tenjin-Minami Station|Tenjin-Minami Station]] 4-minute walk * {{ill|Nishitetsu Bus|de|Nishitetsu Bus|ja|西鉄バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitetsu Bus|1=Nishitetsu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629843}} “Tenjin Kego Shrine – [[:en:Mitsukoshi|Mitsukoshi]] Front” 1-minute walk

{{ill|Kego Park|de|Kego Park|ja|警固公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kego Park|1=Kego Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632311}} borders the northern side of the shrine.
==Access== * [[:en:Nishi-Nippon Railroad|Nishi-Nippon Railroad]] [[:en:Nishitetsu Tenjin Ōmuta Line|Tenjin Omuta Line]] [[:en:Nishitetsu Fukuoka (Tenjin) Station|Nishitetsu Fukuoka (Tenjin) Station]] (South Exit) 1-minute walk * {{ill|Fukuoka City Subway|de|Fukuoka City Subway|ja|福岡市交通局|lt=Fukuoka City Subway|lt_ja=福岡市地下鉄|lt_de=Fukuoka City Subway|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka City Subway|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Fukuoka City Transportation Bureau|WD=Q27576993}} [[:en:Kūkō Line (Fukuoka City Subway)|Airport Line]] [[:en:Tenjin Station|Tenjin Station]] 7-minute walk * Fukuoka City Subway [[:en:Nanakuma Line|Nanakuma Line]] [[:en:Tenjin-Minami Station|Tenjin-Minami Station]] 4-minute walk * {{ill|Nishitetsu Bus|de|Nishitetsu Bus|ja|西鉄バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitetsu Bus|1=Nishitetsu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629843}} “Tenjin Kego Shrine – [[:en:Mitsukoshi|Mitsukoshi]] Front” 1-minute walk
==Photographs== <Gallery> File:Stone monument at Kego-jinja.JPG File:Stone animal monument at Kego-jinja.jpg </Gallery>

07:28, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11632314]] Wikidata name: Kego Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:08, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture}} {{ill|Fukuoka|en|Fukuoka|WD=Q26600|lt=Fukuoka City}} {{ill|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|de|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|ja|福岡市中央区 (福岡市)|lt=Chuo Ward|lt_ja=中央区|lt_de=Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Tenjin 2-2-20 |Latitude degrees = 33 | Latitude minutes = 35 | Latitude seconds = 16 | N (North Latitude) = N |Longitude degrees = 130 | Longitude minutes = 23 | Longitude seconds = 59.7 | E (East Longitude) = E |Map Country Code = JP |Map = Fukuoka City |Enshrined Deities = Kego Ōkami ({{ill|Kannaobi no Kami|de|Kannaobi no Kami|ja|神直毘神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannaobi no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神直日神|1=Kamuna'obi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q86725467}} ・ {{ill|Kannaobi no Kami|de|Kannaobi no Kami|ja|直毘神|lt=Great Kannaobi no Kami|lt_ja=大直毘神|lt_de=Kannaobi no Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannaobi no Kami|1=Naobinokami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581794|1=Ōnaobi no Kami}} ・ {{ill|Yasomagatsuhinokami|de|Yasomagatsuhinokami|ja|禍津日神|lt=Yasomagatsuhinokami|lt_ja=八十禍津日神|lt_de=Yasomagatsuhinokami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasomagatsuhinokami|1=Magatsuhinokami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591203|1=Magatsuhi-no-kami}}) |Founded = {{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|WD=Q179971|lt=Emperor Chūai}} 9th year |Status = Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|de|Präfektur-Schrein|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Festival = Saturdays and Sundays after {{ill|July 19|en|July 19|WD=Q2726|lt=July 19}} (Natsugoshi Festival) |location = {{ill|Tenjin|WD=Q603281}} |coordinates = {{Coord|33.58761111111111|N|130.3996111111111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Magatsuhinokami|WD=Q11591203|1=Magatsuhi-no-kami}}; {{ill|Naobinokami|WD=Q11581794|1=Ōnaobi no Kami}} }} {{osm box|n|384542332}}
'''Kego Shrine''' (けごじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture}} {{ill|Fukuoka|en|Fukuoka|WD=Q26600|lt=Fukuoka City}} {{ill|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|de|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|ja|福岡市中央区 (福岡市)|lt=Chuo Ward|lt_ja=中央区|lt_de=Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} located at Tenjin 2-2-20, and is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shrine}}. Its former shrine Rank was {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|de|Präfektur-Schrein|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.
==Enshrined Deities== Kego Ōkami (the three deities): * {{ill|Yasomagatsuhinokami|de|Yasomagatsuhinokami|ja|禍津日神|lt=Yasomagatsuhinokami|lt_ja=八十禍津日神|lt_de=Yasomagatsuhinokami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasomagatsuhinokami|1=Magatsuhinokami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591203|1=Magatsuhi-no-kami}} (yasomagatsuhinokami) * {{ill|Kannaobi no Kami|de|Kannaobi no Kami|ja|直毘神|lt=Kannaobi no Kami|lt_ja=神直毘神|lt_de=Kannaobi no Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannaobi no Kami|1=Naobinokami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581794|1=Ōnaobi no Kami}} (kannaobi no kami) * {{ill|Kannaobi no Kami|de|Kannaobi no Kami|ja|直毘神|lt=Great Kannaobi no Kami|lt_ja=大直毘神|lt_de=Great Kannaobi no Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannaobi no Kami|1=Naobinokami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581794|1=Ōnaobi no Kami}} (great kannaobi no kami)
==History== According to shrine legend, during the {{ill|Conquest of the Three Han|de|Eroberung der Drei Han|ja|三韓征伐|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Conquest of the Three Han|1=Silla–Wa War|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11249435}}, Empress Jingū’s fleet was protected and led to victory by Kego Ōkami, who was then enshrined at Fukasaki (around the area of the main bailey of {{ill|Fukuoka Castle|en|Fukuoka Castle|WD=Q1143944|lt=Fukuoka Castle}}), marking the beginning of the shrine. In the 6th year of the {{ill|Keichō|en|Keichō|WD=Q1328185|lt=Keichō era}} ({{ill|1601|en|1601|WD=Q6725|lt=1601}}), during the construction of Fukuoka Castle, the shrine was moved to Shimokego Village (in the area around present-day {{ill|Fukuoka|en|Fukuoka|WD=Q26600|lt=Fukuoka City}}, {{ill|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|de|Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|ja|福岡市中央区 (福岡市)|lt=Chuo Ward|lt_ja=中央区|lt_de=Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka City Chuo Ward|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Tenjin (kami)|en|Tenjin (kami)|WD=Q1753428|lt=Tenjin}})). In the 13th year of the {{ill|Keichō|en|Keichō|WD=Q1328185|lt=Keichō era}} ({{ill|1608|en|1608|WD=Q6758|lt=1608}}), {{ill|Kuroda Nagamasa|en|Kuroda Nagamasa|WD=Q950496|lt=Kuroda Nagamasa}}, the lord of Fukuoka Castle, had a shrine building erected at its current location. In the 5th year of the {{ill|Taishō era|en|Taishō era|WD=Q329174|lt=Taishō era}} ({{ill|1916|en|1916|WD=Q2087|lt=1916}}), it was Ranked as a Prefectural Shrine.
The shrine’s name and the surrounding area “Kego” derive from the former government office “Kegosho” that was once located in the {{ill|Kōrokan|en|Kōrokan|WD=Q11675677|lt=Kōrokukan}}.
==Facilities== [[File:Haiden of Kego Shrine 2.jpg|thumb|240px|{{ill|Haiden (Shinto)|en|Haiden (Shinto)|WD=Q3125745|lt=Haiden}}]] [[File:Shimmon Gate of Kego Shrine.JPG|thumb|240px|{{ill|Shinmon Gate|de|Shinmon Gate|ja|神門|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmon Gate|1=Shinmon Gate|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591017}}]] * Haiden (Worship Hall) * Honden (Main Hall) * Great Torii * Shinmon Gate * Harinoki Monument * Ima Masu Inari Shrine * Shintoku Hall (rental venue)
{{ill|Kego Park|de|Kego Park|ja|警固公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kego Park|1=Kego Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632311}} borders the northern side of the shrine.
==Access== * {{ill|Nishi-Nippon Railroad|en|Nishi-Nippon Railroad|WD=Q869251|lt=Nishi-Nippon Railroad}} {{ill|Nishitetsu Tenjin Ōmuta Line|en|Nishitetsu Tenjin Ōmuta Line|WD=Q901075|lt=Tenjin Omuta Line}} {{ill|Nishitetsu Fukuoka (Tenjin) Station|en|Nishitetsu Fukuoka (Tenjin) Station|WD=Q901130|lt=Nishitetsu Fukuoka (Tenjin) Station}} (South Exit) 1-minute walk * {{ill|Fukuoka City Subway|de|Fukuoka City Subway|ja|福岡市交通局|lt=Fukuoka City Subway|lt_ja=福岡市地下鉄|lt_de=Fukuoka City Subway|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka City Subway|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Fukuoka City Transportation Bureau|WD=Q27576993}} {{ill|Kūkō Line (Fukuoka City Subway)|en|Kūkō Line (Fukuoka City Subway)|WD=Q908188|lt=Airport Line}} {{ill|Tenjin Station|en|Tenjin Station|WD=Q1039182|lt=Tenjin Station}} 7-minute walk * Fukuoka City Subway {{ill|Nanakuma Line|en|Nanakuma Line|WD=Q1039166|lt=Nanakuma Line}} {{ill|Tenjin-Minami Station|en|Tenjin-Minami Station|WD=Q4357192|lt=Tenjin-Minami Station}} 4-minute walk * {{ill|Nishitetsu Bus|de|Nishitetsu Bus|ja|西鉄バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitetsu Bus|1=Nishitetsu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629843}} “Tenjin Kego Shrine – {{ill|Mitsukoshi|en|Mitsukoshi|WD=Q1065598|lt=Mitsukoshi}} Front” 1-minute walk
==Photographs== <Gallery> File:Stone monument at Kego-jinja.JPG File:Stone animal monument at Kego-jinja.jpg </Gallery>
==External Links== {{Commonscat|Kego-jinja}}

Shrine|de|Präfektur-Schrein|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Festival = Saturdays and Sundays after {{ill|July 19|en|July 19|WD=Q2726|lt=July 19}} (Natsugoshi Festival) |location =
